MP Rajya Shiksha Kendra RSK Class 5th & 8th Result 2026

Madhya Pradesh Class V & VIII Results 2026 - Short Details of Notification

OnlyJobs : WWW.OnlyJobs

Important Dates

  • Class 5th Result Available : 25/03/2026
  • Class 8th Result Available :25/03/2026
  • Result Declared Time : 01:30 PM

Exam Conducted By

  • Board of Secondary Education, Madhya Pradesh, MPBSE Bhopal Are Conducted the Class 5th and Class 8th Annual Exam 2026.

Madhya Pradesh Class 5th and 8th Results 2026: Details

  • Approx 12 Lakh children have participated in the class 5th examination 2026 of Madhya Pradesh.
  • Approx 11 Lakh children have participated in the class 8th examination 2026 of Madhya Pradesh.
  • There are a total of 23 lakh Approx children who are waiting for the results of the examination which is end .
  • If we talk about the results of class 5 and class 8 of Madhya Pradesh year 2023, then 82.7 percent candidates passed in class 5th and 76.09 percent candidates passed in class 8th.
  • This year 2026 the percentage of exam result will be known to the candidates after the release of the result.

MP Result 2026 MP Board Class 5th & 8th Result How to Check

  • Students who are registered in Madhya Pradesh Board Class 5 and Class 8 examination can check their result online on 25 March 2026 after 01:30 PM.
  • Madhya Pradesh Board Class 5 and Class 8 exams 2026 are organized by the Madhya Pradesh Board.
  • The Madhya Pradesh Board Class 5 and Class 8 exam results will be released on the official website of Rajya Shiksha Kendra at rskmp.in on 25  March 2026 at 01:30 PM.
  • In Madhya Pradesh Class 5 and Class 8 Exam Result 2026, students of all government schools of Madhya Pradesh state can check their result, subject wise also result and its number will be available.
  • To check the Madhya Pradesh Class 5 Annual Exam Result 2026, the candidate must have a roll number. After the release of the result, you can see the result by entering your roll number on the portal.
  • To check the Madhya Pradesh Class 8 Annual Exam Result 2026, the candidate must have a roll number. After the release of the result, you can see the result by entering your roll number on the portal.
  • The direct link to view the Madhya Pradesh Class 5 Annual Exam 2026 and Class 8 Annual Exam 2026results is also available on the OnlyJobs website.

PNB Apprentices Result 2026 OUT (Direct Link) - Download Provisional List @pnb.bank.in

Quick Summary: Punjab National Bank (PNB) has released PNB Apprentices Result 2026 on 27th March 2026 at official portal pnb.bank.in. Candidates can download the Provisional List of Selected Apprentices using the direct link. Check qualifying status, merit details, and next selection process below.

Are you waiting for PNB Apprentices Result 2026? Great news! Punjab National Bank has officially published the provisional result for Apprentices posts today (27th March 2026). Thousands of candidates who appeared in the online written examination held on 8th March 2026 can now verify their qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about PNB Apprentices Result 2026 including direct download links, provisional selected list, expected next steps, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

PNB Apprentices 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Punjab National Bank (PNB)
Post Name Apprentices
Result Type Provisional List of Selected Candidates (Written Examination)
Result Declaration Date 27th March 2026
Exam Conducted On 8th March 2026
Selection Stages Online Written Test → Local Language Test → Medical Exam & Document Verification
Official Website pnb.bank.in

Check Your Result Now

Download PNB Apprentices Result 2026

Direct official link - Provisional List of Selected Apprentices (PDF)

How to Check PNB Apprentices Result 2026?

Candidates can check the PNB Apprentices Result 2026 by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website pnb.bank.in
  2. Click on the Recruitments / Careers section
  3. Look for the notice titled "27.03.2026 - Provisional List of Selected Apprentices as per Written Test on 08.03.2026"
  4. Download the PDF file
  5. Search your Roll Number / Name in the provisional selected list

PNB Apprentices Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The PNB Apprentices provisional list 2026 contains details of provisionally shortlisted candidates based on the online written test. Separate considerations may apply for categories.

Provisional List Contains:

  • Roll Numbers / Registration Numbers of shortlisted candidates
  • Candidate Names
  • Category details
  • Qualifying status for next stages

Next Steps After Provisional List:

  1. Local Language Test
  2. Medical Examination
  3. Document Verification
  4. Final Engagement as Apprentice

PNB Apprentices Scorecard / Result 2026 - Information Breakdown

The provisional result primarily shows shortlisting status. Individual scorecards (if released separately) would typically display:

Candidate Name As per application
Roll Number / Reg. No. Unique identification
Exam Date 8th March 2026
Qualifying Status Provisionally Shortlisted / Not Shortlisted

Important Instructions for Shortlisted Candidates

  • ✓ Download and save the provisional list PDF immediately
  • ✓ Take printouts for future reference
  • ✓ Keep checking official website for Language Test / Medical call letters
  • ✓ Prepare required documents for verification
  • ✓ Verify all personal details - report discrepancies promptly
  • ✓ Stay updated via registered email and mobile for further instructions

The Computer Based Test (CBT) was conducted on 30th January 2026. Candidates can download post-wise merit list PDFs from the official portal aiimsgorakhpur.edu.in. Check qualifying status, marks, and next selection process (Document Verification) below.

Are you waiting for AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Result 2026? Great news! AIIMS Gorakhpur has officially published the results for various Non-Faculty posts (Total 69 vacancies) shortly after the CBT held on 30th January 2026. Thousands of candidates who appeared in the examination can now check their merit list and qualifying status online.

This article provides complete information about AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Result 2026 including direct download links, post-wise merit lists, selection process, important instructions, and step-by-step guidance to check results.

AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty 2026 - Result Dashboard

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) Gorakhpur
Post Name Various Non-Faculty Group A, B & C Posts (Tutor/Clinical Instructor, Assistant Administrative Officer, Junior Accounts Officer, Store Keeper, Jr. Physiotherapist, Technical Assistant (ENT), Optometrist, Technician (Radiology), Technician (Radiotherapy), Operation Theatre Assistant, Junior Medical Lab Technologist, Pharmacist, Medical Record Technician, Mortuary Attendant & others)
Result Type CBT Written Examination Result / Merit List
Result Declaration Date Shortly after 30th January 2026 (OUT Now)
Exam Conducted On 30th January 2026
Selection Stages CBT → Document Verification → Skill Test/Interview (where applicable)
Official Website aiimsgorakhpur.edu.in

Check Your Result Now

Download AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Result 2026

Direct official link - Post-wise Merit List PDFs available

How to Check AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Result 2026?

Candidates can download their AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Result 2026 merit list by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: aiimsgorakhpur.edu.in
  2. Go to the "Current Notices" or "Current Result" section
  3. Find the link for "Result of Non-Faculty Posts" or specific post merit list
  4. Click on the relevant post-wise PDF link
  5. Search your Roll Number / Name in the merit list PDF
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Merit List 2026 - What's Inside?

The AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty merit list 2026 is prepared post-wise and category-wise based on CBT performance. Separate merit lists are available for each post.

Merit List Contains:

  • Roll Number of candidates
  • Candidate Name
  • Category (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS/PwBD)
  • Marks Obtained in CBT
  • Qualifying Status (Qualified/Not Qualified)
  • Rank/Position in the list

Types of Merit Lists Released:

  1. Post-wise Merit List: Separate for each Non-Faculty post

OnlyJobs Home page

This document is published and shall be construed in accordance with the provisions of the Information Technology (reasonable security practices and procedures and sensitive personal data of information) rules, 2011 under Information Technology Act, 2000; that require publishing of the Privacy Policy for collection, use, storage and transfer of sensitive personal data or information.

OnlyJobs Home page

Please read this Privacy Policy carefully by using the Website, you indicate that you understand, agree and consent to this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree with the terms of this Privacy Policy, please do not use this Website.

By providing us your Information or by making use of the facilities provided by the Website, You hereby consent to the collection, storage, processing and transfer of any or all of Your Personal Information and Non-Personal Information by us as specified under this Privacy Policy. You further agree that such collection, use, storage and transfer of Your Information shall not cause any loss or wrongful gain to you or any other person.

USER INFORMATION

To avail certain services on our Websites, users are required to provide certain information for the registration process namely: – a) your name, b) email address, c) sex, d) age, e) PIN code, f) credit card or debit card details g) medical records and history h) sexual orientation, i) biometric information, j) password etc., and / or your occupation, interests, and the like. The Information as supplied by the users enables us to improve our sites and provide you the most user-friendly experience.

All required information is service dependent and we may use the above said user information to, maintain, protect, and improve its services (including advertising services) and for developing new services

Such information will not be considered as sensitive if it is freely available and accessible in the public domain or is furnished under the Right to Information Act, 2005 or any other law for the time being in force.

COOKIES

To improve the responsiveness of the sites for our users, we may use “cookies”, or similar electronic tools to collect information to assign each visitor a unique, random number as a User Identification (User ID) to understand the user’s individual interests using the Identified Computer. Unless you voluntarily identify yourself (through registration, for example), we will have no way of knowing who you are, even if we assign a cookie to your computer. The only personal information a cookie can contain is information you supply (an example of this is when you ask for our Personalised Horoscope). A cookie cannot read data off your hard drive. Our advertisers may also assign their own cookies to your browser (if you click on their ads), a process that we do not control.

Our web servers automatically collect limited information about your computer’s connection to the Internet, including your IP address, when you visit our site. (Your IP address is a number that lets computers attached to the Internet know where to send you data — such as the web pages you view.) Your IP address does not identify you personally. We use this information to deliver our web pages to you upon request, to tailor our site to the interests of our users, to measure traffic within our site and let advertisers know the geographic locations from where our visitors come.

LINKS TO THE OTHER SITES

Our policy discloses the privacy practices for our own web site only. Our site provides links to other websites also that are beyond our control. We shall in no way be responsible in way for
your use of such sites.5.

INFORMATION SHARING

We shares the sensitive personal information to any third party without obtaining the prior consent of the user in the following limited circumstances:

(a) When it is requested or required by law or by any court or governmental agency or authority to disclose, for the purpose of verification of identity, or for the prevention, detection, investigation including cyber incidents, or for prosecution and punishment of offences. These disclosures are made in good faith and belief that such disclosure is reasonably necessary for enforcing these Terms; for complying with the applicable laws and regulations.

(b) We proposes to share such information within its group companies and officers and employees of such group companies for the purpose of processing personal information on its behalf. We also ensure that these recipients of such information agree to process such information based on our instructions and in compliance with this Privacy Policy and any other appropriate confidentiality and security measures.

INFORMATION SECURITY

We take appropriate security measures to protect against unauthorized access to or unauthorized alteration, disclosure or destruction of data. These include internal reviews of our data collection, storage and processing practices and security measures, including appropriate encryption and physical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to systems where we store personal data.

All information gathered on our Website is securely stored within our controlled database. The database is stored on servers secured behind a firewall; access to the servers is password-protected and is strictly limited. However, as effective as our security measures are, no security system is impenetrable. We cannot guarantee the security of our database, nor can we guarantee that information you supply will not be intercepted while being transmitted to us over the Internet. And, of course, any information you include in a posting to the discussion areas is available to anyone with Internet access.

However the internet is an ever evolving medium. We may change our Privacy Policy from time to time to incorporate necessary future changes. Of course, our use of any information we gather will always be consistent with the policy under which the information was collected, regardless of what the new policy may be.

Grievance Redressal

The terms “We” / “Us” / “Our”/”Company” individually and collectively refer to iThink Global Solution. and the terms “You” /”Your” / “Yourself” refer to the users.

This Privacy Policy does not require any physical, electronic or digital signature.

This Privacy Policy is a legally binding document between you and iThink Global Solution.. (both terms defined below). The terms of this Privacy Policy will be effective upon your acceptance of the same (directly or indirectly in electronic form, by clicking on the I accept tab or by use of the website or by other means) and will govern the relationship between you and iThink Global Solution for your use of the website “Website” (defined below).

OnlyJobs Home page

This document is published and shall be construed in accordance with the provisions of the Information Technology (reasonable security practices and procedures and sensitive personal data of information) rules, 2011 under Information Technology Act, 2000; that require publishing of the Privacy Policy for collection, use, storage and transfer of sensitive personal data or information.

OnlyJobs Home page

Please read this Privacy Policy carefully by using the Website, you indicate that you understand, agree and consent to this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree with the terms of this Privacy Policy, please do not use this Website.

By providing us your Information or by making use of the facilities provided by the Website, You hereby consent to the collection, storage, processing and transfer of any or all of Your Personal Information and Non-Personal Information by us as specified under this Privacy Policy. You further agree that such collection, use, storage and transfer of Your Information shall not cause any loss or wrongful gain to you or any other person.

USER INFORMATION

To avail certain services on our Websites, users are required to provide certain information for the registration process namely: – a) your name, b) email address, c) sex, d) age, e) PIN code, f) credit card or debit card details g) medical records and history h) sexual orientation, i) biometric information, j) password etc., and / or your occupation, interests, and the like. The Information as supplied by the users enables us to improve our sites and provide you the most user-friendly experience.

All required information is service dependent and we may use the above said user information to, maintain, protect, and improve its services (including advertising services) and for developing new services

Such information will not be considered as sensitive if it is freely available and accessible in the public domain or is furnished under the Right to Information Act, 2005 or any other law for the time being in force.

COOKIES

To improve the responsiveness of the sites for our users, we may use “cookies”, or similar electronic tools to collect information to assign each visitor a unique, random number as a User Identification (User ID) to understand the user’s individual interests using the Identified Computer. Unless you voluntarily identify yourself (through registration, for example), we will have no way of knowing who you are, even if we assign a cookie to your computer. The only personal information a cookie can contain is information you supply (an example of this is when you ask for our Personalised Horoscope). A cookie cannot read data off your hard drive. Our advertisers may also assign their own cookies to your browser (if you click on their ads), a process that we do not control.

Our web servers automatically collect limited information about your computer’s connection to the Internet, including your IP address, when you visit our site. (Your IP address is a number that lets computers attached to the Internet know where to send you data — such as the web pages you view.) Your IP address does not identify you personally. We use this information to deliver our web pages to you upon request, to tailor our site to the interests of our users, to measure traffic within our site and let advertisers know the geographic locations from where our visitors come.

LINKS TO THE OTHER SITES

Our policy discloses the privacy practices for our own web site only. Our site provides links to other websites also that are beyond our control. We shall in no way be responsible in way for
your use of such sites.5.

INFORMATION SHARING

We shares the sensitive personal information to any third party without obtaining the prior consent of the user in the following limited circumstances:

(a) When it is requested or required by law or by any court or governmental agency or authority to disclose, for the purpose of verification of identity, or for the prevention, detection, investigation including cyber incidents, or for prosecution and punishment of offences. These disclosures are made in good faith and belief that such disclosure is reasonably necessary for enforcing these Terms; for complying with the applicable laws and regulations.

(b) We proposes to share such information within its group companies and officers and employees of such group companies for the purpose of processing personal information on its behalf. We also ensure that these recipients of such information agree to process such information based on our instructions and in compliance with this Privacy Policy and any other appropriate confidentiality and security measures.

INFORMATION SECURITY

We take appropriate security measures to protect against unauthorized access to or unauthorized alteration, disclosure or destruction of data. These include internal reviews of our data collection, storage and processing practices and security measures, including appropriate encryption and physical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to systems where we store personal data.

All information gathered on our Website is securely stored within our controlled database. The database is stored on servers secured behind a firewall; access to the servers is password-protected and is strictly limited. However, as effective as our security measures are, no security system is impenetrable. We cannot guarantee the security of our database, nor can we guarantee that information you supply will not be intercepted while being transmitted to us over the Internet. And, of course, any information you include in a posting to the discussion areas is available to anyone with Internet access.

However the internet is an ever evolving medium. We may change our Privacy Policy from time to time to incorporate necessary future changes. Of course, our use of any information we gather will always be consistent with the policy under which the information was collected, regardless of what the new policy may be.

Grievance Redressal

Redressal Mechanism: Any complaints, abuse or concerns with regards to content and or comment or breach of these terms shall be immediately informed to the designated Grievance Officer as mentioned below via in writing or through email signed with the electronic signature to Arpit Seth (“Grievance Officer”).

The only personal information a cookie can contain is information you supply (an example of this is when you ask for our Personalised Horoscope). A cookie cannot read data off your hard drive. Our advertisers may also assign their own cookies to your browser (if you click on their ads), a process that we do not control.

Our web servers automatically collect limited information about your computer’s connection to the Internet, including your IP address, when you visit our site. And, of course, any information you include in a posting to the discussion areas is available to anyone with Internet access.

However the internet is an ever evolving medium. We may change our Privacy Policy from time to time to incorporate necessary future changes. APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 OUT - Download Hall Ticket at apssb.nic.in

APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 has been released by Arunachal Pradesh Staff Selection Board on 27 March 2026. Candidates who applied for the CLDCE recruitment can download their admit card from the official website apssb.nic.in. This hall ticket is mandatory for appearing in the examination. Read below for the direct download link, step-by-step process, and important instructions for APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026.

APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 - Quick Information

Release Date: 27 March 2026

Download Status: Active Now

Official Website: apssb.nic.in

APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 - Direct Download Link

Download Admit Card Now

Document Status Action
APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 Available Download Now
Official Website Active Visit Portal

APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 Overview

Conducting Body Arunachal Pradesh Staff Selection Board (APSSB)
Exam Name CLDCE 2026
Admit Card Release Date 27 March 2026
Official Website apssb.nic.in

How to Download APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official APSSB website at apssb.nic.in or click the direct download link provided above
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the “Admit Card” or “Latest Announcements” section on the homepage
  3. Step 3: Click on “APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026” link
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials (Application/Registration Number, Date of Birth, Captcha Code if required)
  5. Step 5: Click on “Submit” or “Download Admit Card” button
  6. Step 6: Your APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies)

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to APSSB helpdesk at the earliest.

Details on APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026

Your APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category
  • Gender
  • Application/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: CLDCE 2026
  • Exam Date & Shift (if mentioned)
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day guidelines and rules

Documents Required on APSSB CLDCE Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones and smartphones
  • Smartwatches and digital watches
  • Calculators and electronic devices
  • Books, notes, and study materials
  • Bags and pouches

Important Instructions for APSSB CLDCE Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card immediately
  • Check exam center location
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center well before reporting time
  • Entry closes as per instructions (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow all rules mentioned on the admit card
  • Keep calm and focused

What If APSSB CLDCE Admit Card Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your APSSB CLDCE Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again
  2. Try a different web browser (Chrome recommended)
  3. Check your application status on the official website
  4. Contact APSSB helpdesk immediately for assistance

Important: Act immediately if facing issues. Don’t wait until exam day.

Stay Updated: Bookmark this page for latest updates on APSSB CLDCE Result 2026, answer key, and all recruitment notifications.

VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026 - Latest Update

Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL) and Vardhman Mahaveer Open University (VMOU), Kota have issued the RSCIT admit card for the upcoming 2026 examinations. The exam is being conducted in multiple phases across Rajasthan, and every candidate must carry a printed VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026 to the allotted exam centre.

VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026 – Overview

Particulars Details
Exam Name Rajasthan State Certificate in Information Technology (RSCIT) Exam 2026
Conducting Body Rajasthan Knowledge Corporation Limited (RKCL) & Vardhman Mahaveer Open University (VMOU), Kota
Admit Card Status Released
Download Mode Online Only
Official Admit Card Website Click Here
Other VMOU Website Click Here
RSCIT Exam Dates 29 March 2026 and 12 April 2026
Exam Mode Offline, OMR-Based Exam
Exam Duration 1 Hour

Downloading the VMOU RSCIT admit card is a simple online process and takes only a few minutes. Make sure you keep your exam details ready and follow the steps given below carefully.

  • Open the official RKCL-VMOU portal in your browser: rkcl.vmou.ac.in.

  • On the homepage, click on the link labelled “RSCIT Admit Card 2026” or similar under the examination/notification section.

  • Select your exam district from the list displayed on the screen.

  • Enter your roll number or choose the option to search by name and date of birth.

  • Click on “View / Submit” to generate your admit card.

  • Check all details on the VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026, download the PDF and take a clear A4-size printout for exam day.

Important Links

  • RSCIT Admit Card download: Click Here

  • VMOU official website: Click Here

Details Printed on VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026

After downloading your hall ticket, verify the information mentioned on it to avoid any problem on exam day. The RSCIT admit card generally includes the following details:

  • Candidate’s name, photograph, and signature.

  • Roll number and registration / learner code.

  • Father’s or guardian’s name.

  • Exam date (29 March 2026 or 12 April 2026), exam shift and duration.

  • Reporting time, gate closing time, and exam timing (10:00 AM – 11:00 AM, as per schedule).

  • Exam centre name, code and full address.

  • Important exam day instructions issued by RKCL and VMOU.

VMOU RSCIT Exam Date 2026 and Schedule

The RSCIT exam is being organised in two different phases across Rajasthan to accommodate all registered candidates. As per the latest update, the exam dates are 29 March 2026 and 12 April 2026, both scheduled on Sunday.

The written exam will be conducted in offline mode using an OMR sheet, and the question paper will be available in Hindi and English languages. Candidates must appear at the exam centre mentioned on their VMOU RSCIT admit card; request for change of centre is usually not allowed.

Eligibility to Get VMOU RSCIT Admit Card 2026

Only those learners who have successfully enrolled in the RSCIT course through an authorised RKCL/VMOU centre and whose exam forms are accepted are issued the admit card. If a candidate has completed the course but does not find their admit card on the portal, they should first contact their RSCIT training centre for clarification.

The hall ticket acts as official permission to enter the exam hall, so candidates must keep it safe from the time of download till the exam date.

Important Instructions for VMOU RSCIT Exam Day

To avoid last-minute complications, follow these key instructions on the day of the RSCIT exam.

  • Carry a printed copy of your RSCIT admit card and one original photo ID proof such as Aadhaar card, voter ID or driving licence.

  • Reach the exam centre by the reporting time (around 8:30 AM) to complete verification formalities before gate closure.

  • Do not bring mobile phones, smart watches, calculators or any electronic gadgets into the exam hall.

  • Read all instructions given on the admit card and question paper carefully before starting the exam.

  • In case of any discrepancy in the admit card, inform your centre/official contact before the exam date, not on the exam day itself.

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 has been released online for Undergraduate (UG) semester and end‑semester examinations, and students can download their hall tickets from the official portal using their registration or roll number.

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 - Overview

Purnea University, Bihar issues the Purnea University Admit Card 2026 for BA, BSc, BCom and other UG examinations through its official website and dedicated examination portal. The admit card is compulsory for appearing in the exam and works as your primary identity proof inside the examination hall. Students must download, print, and verify all details on the hall ticket well before the exam date to avoid last‑minute issues.

Key Highlights of Purnea University Admit Card 2026

Detail Information (2026)
University Purnea University, Bihar
Exam Type UG Semester / End‑Semester Examinations 2026
Courses BA, BSc, BCom and other Undergraduate programmes
Academic Session As per respective batch (e.g., 2022‑25, 2024‑28, 2025‑29)
Admit Card Mode Online download only
Official Website Click Here
Admit Card Portal Click Here
Credentials Required Registration number / roll number / user ID and password

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 Release and Exams Covered

Purnea University releases admit cards online a few days before the commencement of each semester or end‑semester examination. Different semesters or parts (Part‑I, Part‑II, Part‑III, Semester‑1, Semester‑2, etc.) may have separate admit card release dates, which are notified through the examination notice section on the university website. Students should regularly check the site and their college notices for updated schedules.

The 2026 admit cards generally cover:

  • UG end‑semester exams for BA, BSc, BCom and allied courses.

  • CBCS semester‑wise exams (such as UG 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th semester) for ongoing batches.

  • Yearly pattern exams like Graduation Part‑III and other notified programmes, depending on the university’s exam calendar.

Important Dates for Purnea University Admit Card 2026

  • Admit card release date (UG end‑semester 2026): Purnea University has released the admit cards for Undergraduate (UG) end‑semester examinations 2026 on 26 March 2026 (around the time the public notice was issued).
  • UG end‑semester exam start date (BA, BSc, BCom, 1st semester, session 2025-29): The revised schedule states that UG first semester (December 2025 session, 2025-29 batch) examinations will start from 30 March 2026.
  • UG end‑semester exam period (1st semester December 2025 exams): Local news reports indicate that the UG first semester December 2025 examinations will be held from 30 March 2026 to 15 April 2026.

Not every student can automatically download the admit card; a few basic conditions apply.

  • Students enrolled in Purnea University or its affiliated colleges in the respective programme and academic session.

  • Candidates who have correctly filled the exam form for their semester/part and whose forms have been accepted by their college and the university.

Pre‑Conditions Before Admit Card Download

  • Submission of the exam form within the deadline.

  • Payment of prescribed examination fees and clearance of dues (if any).

  • Verification and approval of the exam form by the college or examination department.

If any of these steps are incomplete, the admit card may not be visible on the portal.

This is the most important, action‑oriented section for students searching “Purnea University admit card 2026 kaise download kare”.

Direct Links

  • University Website: Click Here

  • Admit Card Direct Link: Click Here

You can place both links near the top of your article to improve click‑through rate and user experience.

  1. Open the official website of Purnea University (https://purneau.ac.in) in your browser.

  2. Go to the “Examination” or “Student Corner” section and click on the latest “Admit Card” or “Download Admit Card” link for your course/semester.

  3. You will be redirected to the admit card portal (examinationpup.in) or a dedicated student login page.

  4. Select your course (BA/BSc/BCom), semester/part and college, if those options appear on the page.

  5. Enter your registration number, roll number, or user ID along with your password/date of birth (as asked on the form).

  6. Click on the “Search”, “Login” or “Submit” button to fetch your hall ticket.

  7. Your Purnea University Admit Card 2026 will appear on the screen with all your exam details.

  8. Download the admit card in PDF format and take one or more clear printouts on A4 paper. Keep one copy for backup in case the first one gets damaged.

If the page does not open, shows an error, or your details are not found, wait for some time and try again or contact your college examination in‑charge.

Details Printed on Purnea University Admit Card 2026

Students should carefully check the information printed on the admit card and get any mistakes corrected before the examination.

Common details include:

  • Student’s name, photograph, and signature.

  • Father’s name and mother’s name.

  • Registration number, roll number, and course name (BA/BSc/BCom etc.).

  • College name, code, and exam session/semester (e.g., UG 1st Semester 2025‑29).

  • List of subjects/papers with paper code, paper name, and exam date.

  • Examination centre name, code, and full address.

  • Reporting time, exam duration, and shift (morning/afternoon/evening).

  • Important instructions and rules for candidates.

If you notice incorrect spellings, wrong subjects, or a wrong exam centre, immediately inform your college or the university examination department.

Important Instructions for Exam Day

Purnea University strictly enforces exam rules, and violation can lead to cancellation of the paper. Your article should highlight the key instructions to add value for students.

What to Carry to the Exam Centre

  • A clear, printed copy of the Purnea University Admit Card 2026.

  • A valid photo identity proof, such as Aadhaar card, voter ID, driving licence, or college ID (if asked by the invigilator).

  • Necessary stationery items - pens, pencils, eraser, and any other items allowed by the university.

What Not to Carry

  • Mobile phones, smart watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic gadgets inside the examination hall.

  • Books, written notes, chits, or any form of study material.

  • Any item considered unfair under university examination rules.

General Exam Rules

  • Reach the exam centre at least 30 minutes before reporting time mentioned on the admit card.

  • Sit only on the seat allotted to you as per the seating chart.

  • Follow all instructions given by invigilators; any kind of malpractice may lead to debarment from the exam.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The MAH MCA CET 2026 admit card has been released today and is now available for State CET Cell, Maharashtra’s official website at cetcell.mahacet.org. Candidates who have successfully completed the application and payment process can log in to the portal to download their hall ticket for the upcoming entrance examination.

MAH MCA CET Admit Card 2026 - Key Details at a Glance

The MAH MCA CET 2026 admit card is the mandatory hall ticket for appearing in the Maharashtra Common Entrance Test for MCA admissions for the academic year 2026-27. The exam is conducted in computer‑based (online) mode, and the admit card contains all essential details such as exam date, time, centre location, and instructions.

Detail Information (2026)
Exam name MAH MCA CET 2026
Exam date 30 March 2026
Admit card release period Fourth week of March 2026 (around 25 March 2026)
Mode of exam Computer‑Based Test (CBT)
Official website Click Here
Conducting authority State Common Entrance Test Cell, Maharashtra

Candidates must verify all information on the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 carefully and keep it safely until counselling and admission are completed.

Downloading the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 is a simple process that every candidate should complete at least a few days before the exam. Here is the step‑by‑step procedure:

  1. Open your browser and visit the official website Click Here.

  2. On the homepage, locate the “CETs” or “Admit Card / Hall Ticket” section and click on the MAH MCA CET 2026 link.

  3. If prompted, select the examination year 2026.

  4. Enter your registered email ID / application number and your password (or date of birth, as applicable).

  5. Click “Log In” or “Submit”, then click “Get Hall Ticket” or “Download Admit Card”.

  6. The MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 will appear on the screen; download the PDF and take 2-3 printouts.

  7. Keep one printout for the exam centre and one backup copy for future reference.

What to Do If the Admit Card Link Is Not Working

If you are unable to download the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 or the link is not opening:

  • Clear your browser cache and try again using Google Chrome or Firefox.

  • Check your internet connection and avoid logging in during peak hours when the site may be slow.

  • Ensure you are entering the correct email / application number and password; use the “Forgot Password” option if needed.

  • If problems persist, contact the State CET Cell helpdesk or check the “Candidate Help & Instructions” section on the portal.

What Details Are Mentioned on MAH MCA CET Admit Card 2026?

The MAH MCA CET hall ticket 2026 contains several important fields that each candidate must verify carefully:

  • Candidate’s name, photograph, and signature.

  • Application number / Registration ID and category details.

  • Exam date, reporting time, and duration.

  • Exam city and centre name with complete address.

  • Important instructions for the exam day, including reporting time, allowed items, and ID proof requirements.

If you find any discrepancy (such as wrong name, photograph, or exam centre), you should immediately contact the CET Cell or follow the grievance‑redressal instructions provided on the official website.

Eligibility and Login Credentials for MAH MCA CET 2026 Admit Card

Only candidates who have successfully filled the MAH MCA CET 2026 application form and completed the payment before the last date are eligible to download the admit card. The admit card will not be generated for incomplete or unpaid applications.

Required login credentials to download the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026:

  • Registered email ID or application number.

  • Password created during registration (or date of birth, if that is the login method).

If you have not completed the application process or missed the payment, you will not be able to log in or download the hall ticket, so it is important to confirm your registration status on cetcell.mahacet.org.

Documents to Carry on MAH MCA CET 2026 Exam Day

On the exam day, candidates must carry the following documents to the test centre:

  • Printed MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 (with signed photograph pasted as per instructions).

  • Original photo ID proof (any one of the following):

    • Aadhaar card / e‑Aadhaar with photograph

    • PAN card

    • Voter ID card

    • Driving licence

    • Bank passbook with photograph

    • Photo‑ID issued by a recognized college or university

Candidates without a valid MAH MCA CET hall ticket and original ID proof may be denied entry to the exam hall, so it is important to keep both documents ready in advance.

Official Links and Important Notices

Always use the official website to avoid fake or phishing sites when downloading the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026:

  • Official portal: Click Here

  • Admit card / Hall ticket section: Look under “CETs → MAH MCA CET 2026 → Admit Card” once the link is enabled.

Exam Schedule and CAP (Counselling) Information

The MAH MCA CET 2026 exam is scheduled for 30 March 2026, and the admit card is expected to be available a few days before the test. After the exam, the result and CAP (Centralised Admission Process) counselling for MCA seats will be conducted in the first week of April and June 2026, respectively.

UPTET Notification 2026 : Age Limit

  • As per rules UPTET
  • Use Tool –

UPTET Online Form 2026 : Educational Qualification 

Class  Eligibility 
Primary Level Teachers (Class 1 to 5)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates are also eligible. In general, a minimum of 50% marks in graduation or the relevant training program is required.
Upper Primary Teacher (Class 6 to 8)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (45% in some cases as per rules) from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as B.Ed, BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates of these courses are also eligible. Additionally, candidates with specialized education qualifications (like Special Education) from RCI-recognized institutes are eligible. In some cases, candidates with 50% marks in intermediate and a 4-year integrated teaching program can also apply.

How To Apply For UPTET Online Form 2026

  • Candidates can apply through link https://upessc.up.gov.in/ or provided below in link section or they can also apply through official site of the UPTET Online Form before Last Date.
  • You Can Check other  Notification Here –

Application Fee

  • General / OBC / EWS : 0/-
  • SC / ST : 0/-
  • No Application Fees

SSC OTR Eligibility

  • One time registration is necessary only for those who want to appear in any SSC recruitment in future. With the help of this registration, the candidate will save a lot of time while applying.
  • No Age Limit in SSC OTR Registration.

SSC Popular Vacancy 

  • SSC Combined Graduate Level CGL Exam
  • SSC Multi Tasking Staff MTS Exam
  • SSC Selection Post Exam
  • SSC Constable GD Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Constable Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Delhi Police Sub Inspector Exam
  • SSC CPO SI Recruitment Exam
  • SSC CHSL 10+2 Recruitment Exam
  • SSC Stenographer Exam
  • SSC Junior Engineer JE Exam
  • SSC Junior Hindi Translator JHT Exam

How to Apply SSC One Time Registration OTR Online Form 2025

  • Staff Selection Commission SSC issued a notice on 22 February 2024 saying that they have launched a new website SSC Gov in, in which all the candidates will have to do fresh one time registration (OTR)
  • Step 1 : Open the Website ssc.gov.in or Direct Link Will be in this Page Some Useful Important Links Section
  • Step 2 : Click on New Registration Tab
  • Step 3 : Students will have to give their basic information such as: Name, Father's Name, Mother's Name, Mobile Number, Email Id and High School Class 10 Roll Number.
  • Step 4 : Verify Your OTR Profile Details & Submit Registration.
  • Step 5 : An OTP will be received on the registered email and registered mobile number of the candidate, he will have to enter it.
  • Step 6 : The registration Part I process will be completed, the candidate will see the OTR Registration No. on the screen.
  • Step 7 : In the next step, you will have to change the default password which has been received on your email, after that you will have to login by writing the registration number along with the password which has been received on the email.
  • Step 9 : Candidates should change the default password of OTR registration number with their custom password. Use capital letters, small letters, numbers and special characters while creating the new password which should be at least 08 digits. Additionally, two security questions will also have to be selected, so that if you forget your password in future, you can recover your password with the help of the security question.
  • Step 10 :  After changing the password, the candidate will have to login again, after that the information about his nationality, category, Visible Identification Mark and information about permanent and local address will be entered in the form.
  • Step 11 : The last step is that here you have to first double check all the information by clicking on the preview button of OTR, after checking, if there is any error then correct it, otherwise click on the declare button and submit the form finally.
  • Step 12 : The registration process of SSC OTR has now been completed, the candidate will be able to see the dashboard whenever any new recruitment comes, or the candidate can also get the information about admit card and result by logging into the SSC dashboard.
  • Our team has also shown a video on how to do SSC one time registration, which you can watch and do your new registration by following the step by step process: SSC OTR Registration Kaise Kare Video Dekhe
  • For more information, you have to complete the notice issued to SSC OTR.

Bank of Baroda – Wealth Management Services (WMS) Deputy Manager, AVP and Other Posts Notification 2026 has been released on 27 March 2026 under advertisement number BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08 on the official website bankofbaroda.bank.in. A total of 104 vacancies have been announced for AVP II – Group Head, AVP II – Territory Head, Deputy Manager – Senior Relationship Manager, and Deputy Manager – Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance) under the Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026.

Candidates within the prescribed age limits (24 to 45 years depending on the post) who hold a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a recognized University, along with the required post‑wise work experience in wealth management, banking, investments or related financial services, are eligible to apply for these Bank of Baroda WMS posts. The online application process for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 starts on 27 March 2026 and will remain open until 16 April 2026, during which candidates must complete registration, form submission and fee payment through the official website.

Candidates can download the detailed Bank of Baroda WMS Notification 2026 PDF and apply online through the direct links provided in this article under the “Current Opportunities” section of the Bank’s Careers page.

Bank of Baroda Deputy Manager, AVP Recruitment 2026 Overview

Advertisement Number BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08
Conducting Authority Bank of Baroda – Wealth Management Services Department
Posts AVP II Group Head, AVP II Territory Head, Deputy Manager Senior Relationship Manager, Deputy Manager Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance)
Total Vacancies 104 posts
Department Wealth Management Services (WMS)

Qualification (basic) Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognized by Govt. of India / Govt. bodies / AICTE; additional PG/management and NISM/IRDA certifications desirable as per post
Age Limit (as on 01.03.2026) AVP II Group Head: 31–45 years; AVP II Territory Head: 27–40 years; Deputy Manager SRM: 24–35 years; Deputy Manager Wealth Strategist: 24–45 years (upper age relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwD/Ex‑servicemen as per rules)
Application Mode Online only through Bank of Baroda official website (Careers → Current Opportunities)

Selection Process Application scrutiny, online test (if conducted), psychometric test or other tests, followed by Personal Interview and final merit based on combined scores
Posting Locations Multiple cities including Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Lucknow, Pune, Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara, Jaipur, Chandigarh and others; transferable anywhere in India

Notification PDF for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 is available on the Bank of Baroda website on the Careers page under “Current Opportunities” with advertisement number BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08. Candidates should download and read the detailed Bank of Baroda WMS Notification 2026 PDF to check post wise eligibility, experience, age limit, selection process and online application instructions before applying.

Document Download Link
Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services 2026 Official Notification PDF Download Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Eligibility Criteria 2026

Candidates applying for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 must carefully check the post wise eligibility criteria related to nationality, age limit, educational qualification, work experience and credit history as on the cut off date 01st March 2026.

Nationality / Citizenship: Applicant must be a citizen of India or a subject of Nepal or Bhutan, or a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1st January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India, or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from specified countries such as Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka and certain East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India, subject to producing a valid certificate of eligibility issued by the Government of India.

Age Limit (as on 01.03.2026): For AVP II – Group Head, the minimum age is 31 years and maximum age is 45 years. For AVP II – Territory Head, the minimum age is 27 years and maximum age is 40 years. For Deputy Manager – Senior Relationship Manager, the minimum age is 24 years and maximum age is 35 years, and for Deputy Manager – Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance), the minimum age is 24 years and maximum age is 45 years, with applicable upper age relaxations for SC, ST, OBC, PwD and Ex‑servicemen as per Government of India norms.

Educational Qualification (Common Requirement): All posts under Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services 2026 require a Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University recognized by the Government of India / Government bodies / AICTE. Desirable qualifications for certain posts include a 2‑year full time Post Graduate Degree / Diploma in Management and relevant Regulatory certifications such as NISM / IRDA, especially for investment and insurance focused roles.

Post-wise Work Experience – AVP II Group Head: Candidates for AVP II – Group Head must have a minimum of 10 years of post‑qualification experience in managing sales in Wealth Management, Retail Banking, Investments or related financial services. Out of this, they should have at least 5 years of experience managing a large team of Relationship Managers and Team Leaders at regional level or equivalent senior leadership capacity.

Post-wise Work Experience – AVP II Territory Head: For the AVP II – Territory Head position, candidates must possess a minimum of 6 years of experience in relationship management in Wealth Management, out of which at least 2 years should be as a Team Leader handling a team of Relationship Managers and driving affluent and high net worth client business.

Post-wise Work Experience – Deputy Manager Senior Relationship Manager: Candidates applying for Deputy Manager – Senior Relationship Manager must have a minimum of 3 years of experience as a Relationship Manager in Wealth Management with Public Sector Banks, Private Banks, Foreign Banks, Broking Firms, Security Firms or Asset Management Companies, with proven ability to acquire and manage HNI and affluent client relationships.

Post-wise Work Experience – Deputy Manager Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance): For the role of Deputy Manager – Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance), candidates must have at least 3 years of experience as a Relationship Manager or Investment Counsellor in Wealth Management, with excellent knowledge of investment products, PMS, AIF, mutual funds and insurance products and strong advisory skills for high value clients.

Experience Conditions: Only post‑qualification experience will be considered and any experience of less than six months in an organization as on 01.03.2026 will not be counted. Experience in clerical cadre will not be considered for determining eligibility for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services professional posts.

Credit History Requirement: Candidates must maintain a satisfactory credit history with a minimum CIBIL score of 680 (or as per bank policy at the time of joining). Applicants with loan or credit card defaults, adverse reports from CIBIL or other credit rating agencies or unsatisfactory credit profiles will be treated as ineligible and their candidature can be rejected at any stage, including after selection.

Other Eligibility Conditions: Candidates must be willing to serve anywhere in India, should meet the prescribed reservation and category criteria with valid SC/ST/OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer)/EWS/PwD certificates, and must be able to produce all original documents related to age, education, work experience and category at the time of interview and document verification. Merely satisfying the minimum eligibility criteria will not confer any right to be called for the online test or personal interview under Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026.

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Salary 2026

Candidates selected through the Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 will be appointed on a fixed term contractual basis in specialist wealth management roles with compensation linked to qualification, experience and market benchmarks.

Remuneration Structure: Bank of Baroda will offer a competitive consolidated remuneration to AVP II – Group Head, AVP II – Territory Head, Deputy Manager – Senior Relationship Manager and Deputy Manager – Wealth Strategist (Investment & Insurance) based on the candidate’s educational qualifications, total and relevant work experience, overall suitability, last drawn salary and prevailing market benchmark for similar wealth management positions.

Contract Tenure & Service Conditions: The engagement as Officer on Contractual Basis (OCB) is for an initial period of 5 years from the date of joining, which may be renewed or extended at the sole discretion of the bank, subject to satisfactory performance, for an additional period of up to 5 more years, but not beyond the candidate’s 60 years of age. All selected candidates will be governed by the bank’s extant policies regarding probation, performance review, termination, and other service conditions applicable to contractual officers.

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Application Fees 2026

Candidates applying online for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026 are required to pay a non‑refundable application fee/intimation charge as per their category through the integrated payment gateway.

Application Fee for UR/EWS/OBC: For candidates belonging to Unreserved (UR), Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Other Backward Classes (OBC), the application fee is Rs. 850/- (inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges, which must be paid online while submitting the Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services online application form 2026.

Application Fee for SC/ST/PwD/ESM/Women: For SC, ST, Persons with Disability (PwD), Ex‑servicemen/Disabled Ex‑servicemen (ESM/DESM) and Women candidates, the fee is Rs. 175/- (intimation charges only, inclusive of GST) plus payment gateway charges. The fee once paid is non‑refundable in all circumstances, whether or not the online test is held or the candidate is shortlisted.

Online Payment Mode: The BOB WMS application fee 2026 must be paid using Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, UPI or other online methods through the payment gateway integrated with the application form. If the online transaction is not successfully completed, candidates must re‑register and make the payment again; multiple payments will not be refunded by the bank.

Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Selection Process 2026

The Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 selection process involves multiple stages including shortlisting, online assessment and interview/psychometric evaluation to identify the most suitable wealth professionals.

Shortlisting & Application Scrutiny: Initially, applications will be scrutinised based on eligibility, educational qualifications, relevant work experience, domain expertise and other criteria decided by the bank. Only shortlisted candidates, as per internal norms, will be called for the next stages, and mere fulfilment of eligibility does not guarantee shortlisting for Bank of Baroda WMS selection 2026.

Online Test & Psychometric Assessment: The selection process may include an online test followed by a psychometric test or other suitable assessments to evaluate the candidate’s personality, communication, problem‑solving ability, efficiency, innovation and willingness to work anywhere in India. The bank reserves the right to conduct or waive the online test and/or psychometric test based on its recruitment strategy.

Personal Interview (PI): Shortlisted candidates will be called for a Personal Interview, where they will be assessed on domain knowledge, relationship management skills, leadership potential (for AVP roles), communication skills and overall suitability for wealth management roles. Candidates must obtain at least 60% marks in PI (55% for reserved categories), or such minimum qualifying marks as may be decided by the bank.

Final Merit & Provisional Selection: The final merit list for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026 will be prepared based on the candidate’s performance in the online test (if conducted), psychometric test and personal interview, as per weightage decided by the bank. Only candidates who qualify in all stages and rank sufficiently high in merit, subject to vacancy and category norms, will be provisionally selected, and all decisions of the bank regarding shortlisting and selection will be final and binding.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026

Interested and eligible candidates must apply online for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 through the Bank of Baroda official website within the specified dates; no other mode of application will be accepted.

Step 1 – Visit Official Website: Go to the Bank of Baroda website www.bankofbaroda.bank.in and navigate to the “Careers → Current Opportunities” section, then click on the link for Recruitment of Professionals on Fixed Term Engagement – Wealth Management Services Department 2026 (Advt. No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/08).

Step 2 – Read Detailed Notification: Download and thoroughly read the Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Notification 2026 PDF to understand the post wise vacancies, eligibility, age limit, experience, selection process, fee, documents required and instructions for filling the online form.

Step 3 – Online Registration: Click on the “Apply Online” link and complete the online registration by providing basic details such as name, mobile number, email ID and creating a registration ID and password. Ensure that the email ID and phone number remain active till the completion of the BOB WMS recruitment process 2026.

Step 4 – Fill Online Application Form: Log in with the generated registration credentials and fill the Bank of Baroda WMS application form 2026 carefully with personal details, educational qualifications, work experience, category, preferred post and location as per records. Verify all entries including spelling of name, date of birth, category and contact details before final submission, as no changes will be allowed after clicking “Submit”.

Step 5 – Upload Photograph, Signature & Documents: Upload a recent passport size photograph, signature and required supporting documents (degree certificates, experience letters, salary breakup, category certificates, etc.) in the prescribed file size and format as specified in the notification and Annexure for document upload guidelines.

Step 6 – Pay Application Fee Online: Proceed to pay the Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services application fee 2026 using Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, UPI or other available options through the secure payment gateway. On successful transaction, an e‑receipt and completed application form will be generated; candidates should save and print both for future reference.

Step 7 – Final Submission & Print: After confirming that all details and fee payment are correct, submit the online application. Keep a printed copy of the system generated application form and fee receipt, as they will be required at the time of personal interview and document verification for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026.

General Instructions for Bank of Baroda WMS Recruitment 2026

Applicants for Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 must adhere to all general instructions issued by the bank to ensure smooth processing of their applications and avoid disqualification at any stage.

Single Valid Application: Candidates should submit only one valid online application for a post; in case of multiple applications, only the latest complete application will be considered and all fees paid for previous registrations will stand forfeited. Multiple appearances for a single post in the selection process will lead to cancellation of candidature.

Accuracy of Information: All information furnished in the Bank of Baroda WMS online application form 2026 must be true, complete and correct. If any discrepancy, false information, or suppression of material facts is found at any stage (before or after appointment), the candidate’s candidature will be cancelled and, if already engaged, services may be terminated without notice.

Document Verification & Category Proof: Candidates must produce original documents (age proof, educational certificates, experience proofs, category certificates for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD, NOC from employer, etc.) along with self‑attested photocopies at the time of interview or whenever called by the bank. No change of category will be permitted after online registration, and results will be processed based on the category declared in the application.

Email & Communication: All communication regarding Bank of Baroda Wealth Management Services Recruitment 2026 including call letters, schedule of tests/interviews, and any updates will be sent to the registered email ID and/or published on the Bank of Baroda website. The bank will not be responsible for non‑receipt of email due to incorrect email ID, spam filtering or technical issues.

Eligibility & Disqualification: Candidates are responsible for ensuring that they meet the eligibility criteria regarding nationality, age, qualification, experience and credit history. Applications from candidates with unsatisfactory credit reports, pending or adverse disciplinary/criminal records, or those not meeting the criteria at any stage are liable to be rejected and no correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

Jurisdiction & Bank’s Decision: Any dispute related to BOB WMS Recruitment 2026 will fall under the jurisdiction of courts/tribunals in Mumbai only. The decisions of Bank of Baroda regarding shortlisting, selection, eligibility, conduct of online test/PI and all other aspects of the recruitment process will be final, binding and not open to challenge by candidates.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Income Tax Department, Office of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, Pune has released the Notice for Inviting Applications for Young Professionals 2026 to engage a pool of talented Young Professionals (YPs) for assisting the Department before the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Pune. This Income Tax Department Young Professional Recruitment 2026 drive offers a total of 2 Young Professional posts on contract basis for one year, extendable by another year based on satisfactory performance, with a consolidated remuneration of Rs. 60,000 per month.

Eligible Indian nationals holding Graduate or Post Graduate degree in Law from recognized universities or qualified Chartered Accountants with at least 50% marks in their degree programme can apply for these Young Professional vacancies in Income Tax Department Pune within the prescribed timeline. Selected Young Professionals will work with CsIT (DR) and Sr. DR in ITAT Pune for case paper book study, judgment analysis, legal research and preparation of briefing notes and written submissions in favour of the department, making this an excellent opportunity for candidates looking for latest taxation and law jobs with the Income Tax Department.

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 – Overview 

Name of Recruitment Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026
Conducting Authority Office of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, Pune
Post Name Young Professional (YP)
Total Vacancies 2 Posts
Job Location Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT), Pune
Contract Period 1 year, extendable by another year subject to satisfactory performance
Monthly Remuneration Rs. 60,000/- (lump sum)
Age Limit Not exceeding 35 years as on the date of advertisement
Educational Qualification Graduate / Post Graduate degree in Law from recognized University or qualified Chartered Accountant with at least 50% marks
Application Mode Offline via Email and Postal submission
Official Website www.incometaxindia.gov.in

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Notification PDF 2026

The detailed Notification PDF for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 is available on the official income tax website under the notices/advertisement section, containing vacancy details, eligibility criteria, remuneration, selection process, and application pro-forma for Young Professionals. Candidates must download and read the Income Tax Young Professional Notification 2026 carefully before filling the application form to ensure that they meet all the required conditions.

 Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 – Important Dates

Last Date to Receive Young Professional Application Form 2026 10.04.2026

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Vacancy 2026 Details

A total of 02 vacancies will be filled for Young Professional (YP) posts under the Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 for engagement in the Office of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax and before the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT), Pune. Candidates can check Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional category wise vacancy details in the table below.

Category Income Tax Young Professional Vacancy 2026
Young Professional 02
Total Income Tax Young Professional Posts 02

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Eligibility Criteria 2026

Candidates applying for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 must carefully check the detailed Young Professional eligibility criteria including age limit, educational qualification and preferred skills before submitting the application form.

Nationality: The applicant must be an Indian national to be eligible for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026.

Age Limit: Candidates should not exceed 35 years of age as on the date of advertisement for Income Tax Young Professional 2026. Relaxation in upper age limit, if any, will be as per Government of India rules as applicable to the Income Tax Department.

Essential Educational Qualification: Candidates must possess a Graduate or Post Graduate degree in Law from recognized Universities, Colleges and Institutions of national or international repute and/or be a qualified Chartered Accountant for Income Tax Young Professional posts.

Minimum Marks Requirement: Candidates should have secured at least 50% marks in their 3-year LLB or 5-year integrated LLB degree programme or in their Post Graduation degree in Law, or must be a qualified Chartered Accountant with the prescribed professional qualification.

Preferred Qualification – Experience in Taxation: Chartered Accountants who have completed their articleship in taxation and Law graduates/Post graduates who have been engaged in research work or projects in taxation will be given preference for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026.

Preferred Skills – ICT & Communication: Candidates having good skills in Information & Communication Technology (ICT) along with strong communication and interpersonal skills shall be preferred for the Young Professional position in Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Pune.

Other Conditions: The Young Professional engagement in Income Tax Department Pune is purely contractual for a period of one year, extendable by another year based on satisfactory performance, and selected candidates will have no right to claim regular employment in the department.

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Selection Process 2026

The Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 selection process will be carried out in two stages as mentioned in the official notification.

Stage 1 – Screening: All applications received for Income Tax Young Professional Recruitment 2026 will first be scrutinized and screened based on eligibility criteria, educational qualification, experience in taxation and preferred skills. Only those candidates who satisfy the prescribed Young Professional eligibility conditions will be shortlisted for the next stage of the selection process.

Stage 2 – Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a personal interview for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional 2026. The intimation for interview will be sent to the candidates on their registered e-mail address and/or by speed post, and no TA/DA will be provided for attending the interview.

Document Verification: At the time of interview, candidates must produce their bio-data and all original certificates in support of age, educational qualification, professional qualification, experience and other claims for verification, failing which they may be disqualified from Income Tax Young Professional selection 2026.

Final Selection: Final selection for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional posts will be based on performance in the interview and overall profile, as per the decision of the competent authority of the Income Tax Department Pune.

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Salary 2026

Candidates appointed through Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 will be engaged on a contractual remuneration basis as specified in the notice.

Monthly Remuneration: Selected Young Professionals will be paid a consolidated salary of Rs. 60,000/- per month (lump sum) during the period of engagement with the Income Tax Department Pune.

Contract Period: The Young Professional engagement will initially be for a period of one year, which may be extended by another year subject to satisfactory performance and requirement of the department.

No Regular Employment Claim: The Young Professional position is purely contractual in nature and does not confer any right on the candidate to claim regular employment or absorption in the Income Tax Department Pune in future.

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Application Fees 2026

The official Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 notice does not mention any specific application fee for submitting the Young Professional application form.

Application Fee Status: Based on the information given in the notification, there is no application fee prescribed for candidates applying for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional posts, and candidates can submit their applications free of cost.

Advisory for Candidates: Aspirants are advised to carefully read the detailed Income Tax Young Professional Notification 2026 on the official website before applying and follow the instructions regarding submission of application form to the specified email and postal address within the stipulated last date.

Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Application Form 2026

The application process for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 is being conducted through offline mode, where eligible candidates must send their duly filled Young Professional application form to the notified email and postal address before the last date. The last date to submit the Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Application Form 2026 is 10.04.2026, and candidates are advised to complete and dispatch their applications well in advance of the closing date.

Candidates have to submit the Income Tax Young Professional application form 2026 by email to [email protected] and also send a hard copy to the Office of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (Judicial), Aaykar Bhawan, 12, Sadhu Vaswani Road, Pune – 411001 as mentioned in the notification.

Click to Apply for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026

How to Apply for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026

Candidates who wish to apply for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026 must submit their applications in the prescribed pro-forma through both email and offline mode within the stipulated last date.

Step 1 – Read the Young Professional Notification: Visit the official Income Tax Department website (www.incometaxindia.gov.in) and carefully read the detailed Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Notification 2026, including eligibility criteria, job description, selection process, and instructions for filling the application form.

Step 2 – Download and Fill Application Form: Download the prescribed pro-forma/application form for Young Professional given in the detailed advertisement and fill in all required details such as personal information, educational qualifications, professional qualifications, work experience, contact details and declaration carefully and correctly.

Step 3 – Attach Supporting Documents: Attach self-attested copies of necessary documents including proof of age, mark sheets and degree certificates of Law/Chartered Accountancy, experience certificates in taxation or research (if any), and any other documents mentioned in the Income Tax Young Professional notification 2026.

Step 4 – Prepare Application for Email Submission: Scan the duly filled and signed Young Professional application form along with relevant supporting documents and compile them into a single PDF file, as per the instructions given in the notification for Income Tax Department Pune Young Professional Recruitment 2026.

Step 5 – Send Application by Email: Email the scanned application form and documents PDF to the notified email add

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) Constable (Tradesman & Driver) Notification 2026 has been released on 21 March 2026 on the official website https://ssb.gov.in/. A total of 827 vacancies have been announced through the SSB Constable Recruitment 2026.

Candidates 18 to 27 years (post-wise age varies as per notification) who have 10th pass / ITI / relevant trade qualification / valid driving licence (for Driver post) / required experience (post-wise) are eligible to apply for these Constable (Tradesman & Driver) vacancies.

All these latest police/defence jobs in SSB are offered in Level-3 of the 7th CPC pay matrix with a salary of ₹21,700–69,100 plus Dearness Allowance, Ration Money Allowance, House Rent Allowance and other central government benefits, making this one of the attractive latest police jobs for 10th pass candidates looking for defence and paramilitary opportunities

The online application process for SSB Constable Recruitment 2026 has started on 21 March 2026 and will remain open until 20 April 2026. Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

SSB Constable Recruitment 2026 – Overview

Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), under the Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India, has released the official Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026 notification for 827 Group ‘C’ Non-Gazetted (Combatised) & Non-Ministerial posts.

Category Group ‘C’ Non-Gazetted (Combatised), Non-Ministerial
Total Vacancies 827 posts
Pay Scale Level-3, ₹21,700–69,100 (7th CPC)
Job Location Anywhere in India / outside India as per SSB requirement
Application Mode Online only
Selection Process PET, PST, Written Exam, Trade/Skill Test, Documentation & Medical Examination

SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for SSB Constable Tradesman & Driver Recruitment 2026 is available in the Employment News (21–27 March 2026) and on the Sashastra Seema Bal recruitment portal under the title “Advertisement for the posts of Constables (Veterinary, Driver, Gardener, Water Carrier, Cobbler, Tailor, Washerman, Barber, Waiter, Carpenter and Nursing Orderly) in Sashastra Seema Bal” with Advertisement No. 524/RC/SSB/Combined Advt./CT to SI (Non-GD)/2025, issued by the Office of the Director General, Sashastra Seema Bal, Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India.
