📄 Complete Details & Notification

GATE COAP 2026 registration has started on the official Common Offer Acceptance Portal (COAP) website coap2026.iitr.ac.in, and eligible GATE 2024, GATE 2025, and GATE 2026 qualified candidates must register online to view and accept IIT M.Tech admission offers for the 2026-27 session.

GATE COAP 2026 Registration - Overview

GATE COAP (Common Offer Acceptance Portal) 2026 is a centralized online platform where GATE‑qualified candidates can see, compare, and accept or reject M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des offers from participating Indian Institutes of Technology. It does not itself grant admission but works as a decision platform that connects your GATE scores to offers made by the IITs.

COAP 2026 - Key Highlights

Detail Information (2026)
Portal Name GATE Common Offer Acceptance Portal (COAP) 2026
Purpose View and accept IIT M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des offers based on GATE
Conducting Institute IIT Roorkee (for COAP 2026)
Academic Year 2026-27
Mode of Registration Online only
Registration Fee No separate COAP registration fee (as per current norms)
Eligible GATE Years GATE 2024, GATE 2025, GATE 2026 (valid scorecard)
Who Can Register Indian citizens with valid GATE score who want IIT PG seats
Official COAP Portal https://coap2026.iitr.ac.in/coap2026/

GATE COAP 2026 Important Dates and Rounds

COAP runs in multiple online rounds, each with its own offer viewing window. Candidates must log in during these windows to check and act on offers.

  • Registration Begins: Mid-March 2026 (Active)
  • Round 1 (View Offers): May 11, 2026 (10 AM) - May 13, 2026 (9 AM)
  • Round 2 (View Offers): May 17, 2026 (10 AM) - May 20, 2026 (9 AM)
  • Round 3 (View Offers): May 24, 2026 (10 AM) - May 27, 2026 (9 AM)
  • Round 4 (View Offers): May 31, 2026 (10 AM) - June 3, 2026 (9 AM)
  • Final Round (Round 10): June 8, 2026 (10 AM) - July 10, 2026 (9 AM) 

GATE COAP 2026 Eligibility Criteria

This section answers “who should register for COAP 2026”.

GATE Score Requirements

  • You must have a valid GATE score in at least one of the following years: 2024, 2025, or 2026.

  • If you have multiple valid GATE scores (for example GATE 2025 and GATE 2026), you should use the scorecard with the higher score or better rank while registering.

Other Basic Conditions

  • COAP is meant for Indian citizens; foreign nationals usually apply directly to IITs without using COAP.

  • COAP registration is compulsory for candidates seeking M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des admission to participating IITs through GATE, because the offers are displayed only through this portal.

  • COAP does not replace the individual IIT application: you still need to fill separate programme‑wise forms on each IIT’s admission portal.

Documents and Details Required for GATE COAP 2026 Registration

Help users prepare everything in advance so registration is smooth.

Details You Must Have

  • GATE Registration/Enrollment ID.

  • GATE paper code, year of exam, and GATE score as per your scorecard.

  • Full name, date of birth, category, and other personal details exactly as in the GATE form.

  • Valid email ID and active mobile number for OTPs and alerts.

Useful Documents to Keep Ready

  • PDF / screenshot of the latest GATE scorecard.

  • A list of IITs and programmes you are planning to apply to (for later use on IIT portals, not at COAP).

How to Register for GATE COAP 2026 (Step‑by‑Step)

This is the main transactional section for “GATE COAP 2026 registration kaise kare”.

Direct COAP 2026 Registration Link

  • COAP 2026 Portal: Click Here

Place this link near the top and again here in your article for better CTR.

Step‑by‑Step GATE COAP 2026 Registration Process

  1. Visit the COAP 2026 portal
    Open your browser and go to the official COAP 2026 website (coap2026.iitr.ac.in/coap2026/).

  2. Click on “Register” / “New User Registration”
    On the home page, click the registration button for new candidates.

  3. Enter GATE details
    Fill in your GATE Registration/Enrollment ID, GATE paper code, year of exam, and GATE score as printed on the scorecard.

  4. Fill personal information
    Enter your name, date of birth, category, email ID, and mobile number exactly as per your GATE records to avoid mismatch issues.

  5. Create login credentials
    Set a strong password, confirm it, and enter the captcha/security code shown on screen.

  6. Submit and verify
    Submit the registration form. Note down the COAP 2026 Registration ID displayed on screen and sent to your email/mobile.

  7. Login to COAP account
    Use your COAP Registration ID and password to log in, verify your details, and keep the credentials safely for use during every round.

You can also add a note:
“There is usually no separate fee for COAP registration; any fee you pay is for the individual IIT application forms, not for COAP itself.”

What to Do After GATE COAP 2026 Registration

Many students are confused after registering, so this section adds real value.

Two Parallel Steps for IIT M.Tech Admission

  • Step 1: Register on COAP (this portal).
    This is required only once and creates your central offer‑view account.

  • Step 2: Apply to individual IITs separately.
    Visit each participating IIT’s admission website (IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Madras, IIT Roorkee, etc.), fill their M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des application forms, pay the institute‑specific fee, and mention your GATE details and COAP registration where asked.

COAP will show offers only from those institutes and programmes where you have already applied directly and been considered based on GATE.

Using COAP 2026 in Every Round

  1. During each active round window, log in to the COAP portal with your Registration ID and password.

  2. Check all the offers displayed from different IITs and programmes (if any).

  3. For each round, choose one of the decision options provided on the portal, commonly:

    • Accept & Freeze - Accept one offer and exit further COAP rounds for that programme.

    • Retain & Wait - Keep your current best offer but remain eligible for upgrade in later rounds.

    • Reject & Wait - Reject the current offer(s) and wait for potential new/ better offers in the next rounds.

  4. Save and submit your choice before that round’s deadline.

  5. After you Accept & Freeze an offer, complete admission formalities (fee payment, document upload, reporting) directly on that IIT’s portal.

GATE COAP 2026 - Official Links and Resources

This section supports navigational intent and trust.

Important Official Links for Candidates

  • COAP 2026 Registration/Login Portal: Click Here

  • GATE Scorecard Download (GATE 2026, 2025, 2024):
    Link on the official GATE website of the organizing IIT for those years.

  • IIT M.Tech Admission Portals:
    Candidates should visit the official admission pages of individual IITs (IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Madras, etc.) to fill programme‑specific forms and pay fees.

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 has been released online for Undergraduate (UG) semester and end‑semester examinations, and students can download their hall tickets from the official portal using their registration or roll number.

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 - Overview

Purnea University, Bihar issues the Purnea University Admit Card 2026 for BA, BSc, BCom and other UG examinations through its official website and dedicated examination portal. The admit card is compulsory for appearing in the exam and works as your primary identity proof inside the examination hall. Students must download, print, and verify all details on the hall ticket well before the exam date to avoid last‑minute issues.

Key Highlights of Purnea University Admit Card 2026

Detail Information (2026)
University Purnea University, Bihar
Exam Type UG Semester / End‑Semester Examinations 2026
Courses BA, BSc, BCom and other Undergraduate programmes
Academic Session As per respective batch (e.g., 2022‑25, 2024‑28, 2025‑29)
Admit Card Mode Online download only
Official Website Click Here
Admit Card Portal Click Here
Credentials Required Registration number / roll number / user ID and password

Purnea University Admit Card 2026 Release and Exams Covered

Purnea University releases admit cards online a few days before the commencement of each semester or end‑semester examination. Different semesters or parts (Part‑I, Part‑II, Part‑III, Semester‑1, Semester‑2, etc.) may have separate admit card release dates, which are notified through the examination notice section on the university website. Students should regularly check the site and their college notices for updated schedules.

The 2026 admit cards generally cover:

  • UG end‑semester exams for BA, BSc, BCom and allied courses.

  • CBCS semester‑wise exams (such as UG 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th semester) for ongoing batches.

  • Yearly pattern exams like Graduation Part‑III and other notified programmes, depending on the university’s exam calendar.

Important Dates for Purnea University Admit Card 2026

  • Admit card release date (UG end‑semester 2026): Purnea University has released the admit cards for Undergraduate (UG) end‑semester examinations 2026 on 26 March 2026 (around the time the public notice was issued).
  • UG end‑semester exam start date (BA, BSc, BCom, 1st semester, session 2025-29): The revised schedule states that UG first semester (December 2025 session, 2025-29 batch) examinations will start from 30 March 2026.
  • UG end‑semester exam period (1st semester December 2025 exams): Local news reports indicate that the UG first semester December 2025 examinations will be held from 30 March 2026 to 15 April 2026.

Not every student can automatically download the admit card; a few basic conditions apply.

  • Students enrolled in Purnea University or its affiliated colleges in the respective programme and academic session.

  • Candidates who have correctly filled the exam form for their semester/part and whose forms have been accepted by their college and the university.

Pre‑Conditions Before Admit Card Download

  • Submission of the exam form within the deadline.

  • Payment of prescribed examination fees and clearance of dues (if any).

  • Verification and approval of the exam form by the college or examination department.

If any of these steps are incomplete, the admit card may not be visible on the portal.

This is the most important, action‑oriented section for students searching “Purnea University admit card 2026 kaise download kare”.

Direct Links

  • University Website: Click Here

  • Admit Card Direct Link: Click Here

You can place both links near the top of your article to improve click‑through rate and user experience.

  1. Open the official website of Purnea University (https://purneau.ac.in) in your browser.

  2. Go to the “Examination” or “Student Corner” section and click on the latest “Admit Card” or “Download Admit Card” link for your course/semester.

  3. You will be redirected to the admit card portal (examinationpup.in) or a dedicated student login page.

  4. Select your course (BA/BSc/BCom), semester/part and college, if those options appear on the page.

  5. Enter your registration number, roll number, or user ID along with your password/date of birth (as asked on the form).

  6. Click on the “Search”, “Login” or “Submit” button to fetch your hall ticket.

  7. Your Purnea University Admit Card 2026 will appear on the screen with all your exam details.

  8. Download the admit card in PDF format and take one or more clear printouts on A4 paper. Keep one copy for backup in case the first one gets damaged.

If the page does not open, shows an error, or your details are not found, wait for some time and try again or contact your college examination in‑charge.

Details Printed on Purnea University Admit Card 2026

Students should carefully check the information printed on the admit card and get any mistakes corrected before the examination.

Common details include:

  • Student’s name, photograph, and signature.

  • Father’s name and mother’s name.

  • Registration number, roll number, and course name (BA/BSc/BCom etc.).

  • College name, code, and exam session/semester (e.g., UG 1st Semester 2025‑29).

  • List of subjects/papers with paper code, paper name, and exam date.

  • Examination centre name, code, and full address.

  • Reporting time, exam duration, and shift (morning/afternoon/evening).

  • Important instructions and rules for candidates.

If you notice incorrect spellings, wrong subjects, or a wrong exam centre, immediately inform your college or the university examination department.

Important Instructions for Exam Day

Purnea University strictly enforces exam rules, and violation can lead to cancellation of the paper. Your article should highlight the key instructions to add value for students.

What to Carry to the Exam Centre

  • A clear, printed copy of the Purnea University Admit Card 2026.

  • A valid photo identity proof, such as Aadhaar card, voter ID, driving licence, or college ID (if asked by the invigilator).

  • Necessary stationery items - pens, pencils, eraser, and any other items allowed by the university.

What Not to Carry

  • Mobile phones, smart watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic gadgets inside the examination hall.

  • Books, written notes, chits, or any form of study material.

  • Any item considered unfair under university examination rules.

General Exam Rules

  • Reach the exam centre at least 30 minutes before reporting time mentioned on the admit card.

  • Sit only on the seat allotted to you as per the seating chart.

  • Follow all instructions given by invigilators; any kind of malpractice may lead to debarment from the exam.

To help users quickly navigate to genuine sources, include all important official links together:

  • Purnea University Official Website: Click Here

  • Admit Card Download Page: Click Here

The MAH MCA CET 2026 admit card has been released today and is now available for State CET Cell, Maharashtra’s official website at cetcell.mahacet.org. Candidates who have successfully completed the application and payment process can log in to the portal to download their hall ticket for the upcoming entrance examination.

MAH MCA CET Admit Card 2026 - Key Details at a Glance

The MAH MCA CET 2026 admit card is the mandatory hall ticket for appearing in the Maharashtra Common Entrance Test for MCA admissions for the academic year 2026-27. The exam is conducted in computer‑based (online) mode, and the admit card contains all essential details such as exam date, time, centre location, and instructions.

Detail Information (2026)
Exam name MAH MCA CET 2026
Exam date 30 March 2026
Admit card release period Fourth week of March 2026 (around 25 March 2026)
Mode of exam Computer‑Based Test (CBT)
Official website Click Here
Conducting authority State Common Entrance Test Cell, Maharashtra

Candidates must verify all information on the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 carefully and keep it safely until counselling and admission are completed.

Downloading the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 is a simple process that every candidate should complete at least a few days before the exam. Here is the step‑by‑step procedure:

  1. Open your browser and visit the official website Click Here.

  2. On the homepage, locate the “CETs” or “Admit Card / Hall Ticket” section and click on the MAH MCA CET 2026 link.

  3. If prompted, select the examination year 2026.

  4. Enter your registered email ID / application number and your password (or date of birth, as applicable).

  5. Click “Log In” or “Submit”, then click “Get Hall Ticket” or “Download Admit Card”.

  6. The MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 will appear on the screen; download the PDF and take 2-3 printouts.

  7. Keep one printout for the exam centre and one backup copy for future reference.

What to Do If the Admit Card Link Is Not Working

If you are unable to download the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 or the link is not opening:

  • Clear your browser cache and try again using Google Chrome or Firefox.

  • Check your internet connection and avoid logging in during peak hours when the site may be slow.

  • Ensure you are entering the correct email / application number and password; use the “Forgot Password” option if needed.

  • If problems persist, contact the State CET Cell helpdesk or check the “Candidate Help & Instructions” section on the portal.

What Details Are Mentioned on MAH MCA CET Admit Card 2026?

The MAH MCA CET hall ticket 2026 contains several important fields that each candidate must verify carefully:

  • Candidate’s name, photograph, and signature.

  • Application number / Registration ID and category details.

  • Exam date, reporting time, and duration.

  • Exam city and centre name with complete address.

  • Important instructions for the exam day, including reporting time, allowed items, and ID proof requirements.

If you find any discrepancy (such as wrong name, photograph, or exam centre), you should immediately contact the CET Cell or follow the grievance‑redressal instructions provided on the official website.

Eligibility and Login Credentials for MAH MCA CET 2026 Admit Card

Only candidates who have successfully filled the MAH MCA CET 2026 application form and completed the payment before the last date are eligible to download the admit card. The admit card will not be generated for incomplete or unpaid applications.

Required login credentials to download the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026:

  • Registered email ID or application number.

  • Password created during registration (or date of birth, if that is the login method).

If you have not completed the application process or missed the payment, you will not be able to log in or download the hall ticket, so it is important to confirm your registration status on cetcell.mahacet.org.

Documents to Carry on MAH MCA CET 2026 Exam Day

On the exam day, candidates must carry the following documents to the test centre:

  • Printed MAH MCA CET admit card 2026 (with signed photograph pasted as per instructions).

  • Original photo ID proof (any one of the following):

    • Aadhaar card / e‑Aadhaar with photograph

    • PAN card

    • Voter ID card

    • Driving licence

    • Bank passbook with photograph

    • Photo‑ID issued by a recognized college or university

Candidates without a valid MAH MCA CET hall ticket and original ID proof may be denied entry to the exam hall, so it is important to keep both documents ready in advance.

Official Links and Important Notices

Always use the official website to avoid fake or phishing sites when downloading the MAH MCA CET admit card 2026:

  • Official portal: Click Here

  • Admit card / Hall ticket section: Look under “CETs → MAH MCA CET 2026 → Admit Card” once the link is enabled.

Exam Schedule and CAP (Counselling) Information

The MAH MCA CET 2026 exam is scheduled for 30 March 2026, and the admit card is expected to be available a few days before the test. After the exam, the result and CAP (Centralised Admission Process) counselling for MCA seats will be conducted in the first week of April and June 2026, respectively.

Trades AC Mechanic, Carpenter, COPA, Diesel Mechanic, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Fitter, Industrial Electronics, Machinist, MMTM, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Painter, Welder
Training Units Workshops, Divisions and Sheds of Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Personnel and S&T Departments under SCR (e.g., Lallaguda, Rayanapadu, Tirupati, Secunderabad, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Guntakal, Guntur, Nanded etc.)
Training Year Apprenticeship Training Programme for the year 2025-26
Educational Qualification 10th class (10+2 system) with minimum 50% aggregate marks and ITI in relevant trade from NCVT/SCVT recognised institute
Age Limit 15 to 24 years as on 11.04.2026 (upper age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC-NCL/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen as per rules)
Selection Process Merit list based on average of 10th marks and ITI marks, no written test or viva-voce
Application Mode Online only through RRC/SC webpage
Application Fee ₹100 for UR/OBC/EWS males; No fee for SC/ST, female/Transgender and PwBD candidates
Official Website https://scr.indianrailways.gov.in → About Us → RRC-SCR

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 is available on the RRC/SCR page of the South Central Railway website under the title “NOTIFICATION FOR ENGAGEMENT OF ACT APPRENTICES (2025-26) UNDER APPRENTICE ACT, 1961” with file number SCR/P-HQ/RRC/111/Act. App/2025-26 dated 11.03.2026.

Document Download Link
SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 Official Notification PDF Download Notification PDF

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 Vacancy Details 

Trade Total
AC Mechanic 251
Carpenter 22
COPA 181
Diesel Mechanic 105
Electrician 1258
Electronic Mechanic 88
Fitter 539
Industrial Electronics 33
Machinist 54
MMTM 24
Motor Mechanic 12
Painter 32
Welder 202
TOTAL 2801

South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26 - Important Dates

Notification Release Date 11 March 2026
Online Application Start Date 12 March 2026, 17:00 hrs
Last Date to Apply Online 11 April 2026, 23:59 hrs
Cut-off Date for Age Calculation 11 April 2026
SCR Apprentice Merit List & Document Verification Dates To be notified on RRC/SCR website

Eligibility criteria - South Central Railway Act Apprentice 2025-26

1) Educational qualification​

  • 10th class/SSC passed under 10+2 system with minimum 50% aggregate marks from a recognised board.

  • ITI certificate in the relevant notified trade from an institute recognised by NCVT/SCVT (trade-wise: AC Mechanic, Carpenter, COPA, Diesel Mechanic, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Fitter, Industrial Electronics, Machinist, MMTM, Motor Mechanic Vehicle, Painter, Welder).

  • Candidate must possess required qualification as on date of online application submission.

  • Engineering graduates and diploma holders are not eligible for this Act Apprentice notification (covered under separate scheme).

2) Age limit (as on 11.04.2026)​

  • Minimum age: 15 years.

  • Maximum age: 24 years.

Category-wise permissible date of birth range:

Category Date of birth between
UR / EWS 11-04-2002 and 11-04-2011
SC / ST 11-04-1997 and 11-04-2011
OBC-NCL 11-04-1999 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (UR) 11-04-1992 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (SC/ST) 11-04-1987 and 11-04-2011
Ex-Servicemen / PwBD (OBC) 11-04-1989 and 11-04-2011

Relaxations included in above ranges:

  • SC/ST: +5 years.

  • OBC-NCL: +3 years.

  • PwBD: +10 years.

  • Ex-Servicemen: +10 years up to length of service in Defence Forces (no extra relaxation for their children).

3) Residential / jurisdiction eligibility​
Candidates must reside in districts under South Central Railway jurisdiction and provide valid proof of residence. Eligible areas:

  • Telangana: all districts.

  • Andhra Pradesh: all districts except Manyam, Srikakulam, Vizianagaram.

  • Maharashtra: Akola, Amaravati, Aurangabad, Beed, Chandrapur, Hingoli, Jalna, Latur, Nanded, Nasik, Parbhani, Washim, Yavatmal.

  • Karnataka: Balki, Bellari, Belgaum, Bidar, Gulbarga/Kalaburgi, Raichur, Yadgir.

  • Tamil Nadu: Vellore.

  • Madhya Pradesh: Burhanpur, Khandwa.

4) Category certificates (for reservation)​

  • SC/ST: Central Government format caste certificate (Annexure-B).

  • OBC-NCL: caste + NCL certificate (issued within 3 years) in Central format (Annexure-C) + self-declaration (Annexure-D).

  • EWS: valid Income & Asset certificate (Annexure-E).

  • PwBD: disability ≥40% in prescribed format (Annexure-G/H).

5) Salary/Stipend

  • Selected candidates will be paid a stipend during the apprenticeship at the prescribed rates as per extant instructions.

SCR Apprentice Selection Process 2025-26

  • Merit List Basis: Selection for SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 will be done purely on merit, by taking the average of percentage marks in 10th (minimum 50% aggregate) and ITI marks, giving equal weightage to both.
  • No Exam / Interview: There will be no written test or viva-voce for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26.
  • Tie-breaking: If two candidates have the same aggregate percentage, older candidate (higher age) will be preferred; if DOB is also same, the candidate who passed Matriculation earlier will be preferred.
  • Document Verification (DV): Shortlisted candidates will be called for DV where all original certificates (education, ITI, caste, EWS, PwBD, ExSM, RDAT registration, medical fitness) will be verified.
  • Medical Fitness: Candidates must produce medical fitness certificate from a government-authorised doctor (Gazetted, not below Asst. Surgeon) in prescribed format at DV; without this, they will not be considered.
  • Training & Stipend: Selected candidates will undergo apprenticeship training as per Central Apprenticeship Council syllabus and receive stipend at prescribed rates during training.
  • No Job Guarantee: SCR apprenticeship 2025-26 provides training only; there is no guarantee of regular railway job after completion of apprenticeship.

South Central Railway Apprentice Application Form 2026

  • Application Mode: Online only via RRC/SCR portal; no offline forms will be accepted.
  • Application Dates: SCR Apprentice online form 2026 is available from 12 March 2026 (17:00 hrs) to 11 April 2026 (23:59 hrs).
  • Fee: ₹100 for applicable categories; SC/ST, female/Transgender and PwBD candidates are exempt from fee payment.
  • Multiple Trades: Candidates with ITI in more than one trade can submit separate SCR Apprentice application 2026 for each trade.
  • Communication: All updates regarding SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 application form, merit list and DV will be sent via registered email/SMS and uploaded on RRC-SCR webpage only.

How to Apply for South Central Railway Apprentice Recruitment 2025-26

  1. Visit the official South Central Railway website: https://scr.indianrailways.gov.in and go to About Us → RRC-SCR to open the ONLINE ACT APPRENTICE APPLICATION link.
  2. Read the detailed SCR Act Apprentice 2025-26 notification carefully and ensure you belong to the eligible states/districts and possess required 10th + ITI qualification.
  3. Complete Registration: select state and district, upload address proof, enter valid mobile number and email ID, create a password and verify OTPs sent to mobile and email.
  4. Login as a registered candidate and fill in personal details (name, DOB, category, etc.) and educational/technical details (10th marks, ITI trade, ITI marks, RDAT registration number).
  5. Select your priority/preference of training units (Divisions/Workshops/Sheds) only for trades you are eligible for; options once submitted cannot be changed.
  6. Upload a recent colour photograph (JPEG, ≤1 MB, not older than 3 months), signature image (≤1 MB) and required certificates (10th, ITI, RDAT form, caste, EWS, PwBD, ExSM, medical certificate) in PDF format (≤2 MB).
  7. Ensure you have completed RDAT/NAPS registration at www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in and obtained registration number before filling the SCR apprentice application.
  8. Check all details in the Preview page carefully; after clicking Submit, no modification will be allowed.
  9. Pay the application fee (if applicable) through online payment gateway (Net Banking/Debit Card/Credit Card/UPI) and generate e-receipt.
  10. Download/print the final submitted SCR Apprentice application form 2026 and keep it along with fee receipt and uploaded documents for Document Verification.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) Head Constable Notification 2026 has been released in the Employment News (21–27 March 2026) on the official website recruitment.ssb.gov.in. A total of 233 vacancies have been announced through the SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 for the posts of Head Constable (Steward), Head Constable (Communication), Head Constable (Electrician), and Head Constable (Veterinary).

Candidates 18–27 years (Steward, Communication, Veterinary) and 18–25 years (Electrician) who have 12th Pass / Matriculation with Diploma, Certificate Course, or relevant work experience depending on the post are eligible to apply for these Head Constable vacancies. The online application process for SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 will begin as per the Employment News publication date, and candidates must submit their applications within 30 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in Employment News.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided in this article.

SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 Overview

Company Name Office of the Director General, Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
Post Name Head Constable (Steward), Head Constable (Communication), Head Constable (Electrician), Head Constable (Veterinary)
No of Posts 233
Salary Level-4 Pay Matrix: ₹25,500/- to ₹81,100/- Per Month (Plus DA, RA, HRA and other admissible allowances)
Qualification Post-wise: 12th Pass / Matriculation + Diploma / Certificate Course / Working Experience as applicable
Age Limit 18–27 Years (Steward, Communication, Veterinary) | 18–25 Years (Electrician)
Start Date for Apply As per Employment News Publication Date
Last Date for Apply 30 days from the date of publication of this advertisement in Employment News
Official Website recruitment.ssb.gov.in

SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification PDF for SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 is available in the Employment News (21–27 March 2026) and on the Sashastra Seema Bal recruitment portal under the title “Advertisement for the posts of Head Constable (Steward, Communication, Electrician, Veterinary ) in Sashastra Seema Bal” with Advertisement No. 524/RC/SSB/Combined Advt./CT to SI (Non-GD)/2025, issued by the Office of the Director General, Sashastra Seema Bal, Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India.

Download Employment News Notification PDF

SSB Head Constable Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

Sl. No. Post Name Total
1 Head Constable (Steward) 2
2 Head Constable (Communication) 197
3 Head Constable (Electrician) 29
4 Head Constable (Veterinary) 5
Grand Total 233

UR: Un-Reserved | EWS: Economically Weaker Section | SC: Scheduled Caste | ST: Scheduled Tribe | OBC: Other Backward Class.
10% vacancies are reserved for Ex-Servicemen.

Eligibility Criteria

  • Qualifications – Head Constable (Steward):
    • 12th class pass from a recognized Board.
    • Diploma of minimum two years duration in Catering and Kitchen Management from an institution recognized by the Central Government or State Government.
    • Working experience of minimum one year as a Steward in a minimum hundred bedded hospital or institution recognized by the Central Government or State Government.
  • Qualifications – Head Constable (Communication):
    • 12th class pass in Science with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics from a recognized Board; OR
    • Three years Diploma in Electronics or Communications or Computer Science or Information Technology from a recognized Institute.
  • Qualifications – Head Constable (Electrician):
    • Matriculation or equivalent from a recognized Board.
    • Two years work experience in respective trade; OR
    • One year certificate course from a recognized Industrial Training Institute or Vocational Institute with at least one year experience in the respective trade; OR
    • Two years Diploma from a recognized Industrial Training Institute in the respective trade or similar trade; AND
    • Must qualify trade test.
  • Qualifications – Head Constable (Veterinary):
    • 12th class pass with Science and Biology as main subject from a recognized Board.
    • Two years Diploma Course in Veterinary and Livestock Development or Veterinary Stock Assistant Course or Animal Husbandry Course from an institution recognized by the Central Government or State Government.
  • Nationality: Only Indian citizens are eligible. No person who is not a citizen of India shall be appointed except with prior written permission of the Central Government. Subjects of Nepal or Bhutan may be appointed in the Force.
  • Crucial Date for Age & Eligibility: The closing date for receipt of applications i.e. 30 (thirty) days from the date of publication of this advertisement in the Employment News will be the crucial date to determine age and all eligibility criteria.
  • Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions for the applied post and be in possession of all certificates as on the last date of receipt of applications.

Salary / Stipend

  • Post: Head Constables (Steward, Communication, Electrician and Veterinary)
  • Pay Scale (7th CPC): Level-4 Pay Matrix – Basic Pay ₹25,500/- to ₹81,100/- Per Month
  • Other Allowances: Dearness Allowance, Ration Money Allowance, House Rent Allowance and any other allowances as admissible in SSB from time to time as per Government orders.
  • Pension: Selected candidates will be covered under Defined Contributory Pension Scheme (New Pension Scheme) OR Unified Defined Contributory Pension Scheme (UPS) as applicable for new entrants to Central Government services w.e.f. 01st January 2004 or 01st April 2025 respectively, as per prevailing government rules and organizational policies.

Age Limit 

  • Head Constable (Steward): Between 18 and 27 Years
  • Head Constable (Communication): Between 18 and 27 Years
  • Head Constable (Electrician): Between 18 and 25 Years
  • Head Constable (Veterinary): Between 18 and 27 Years
  • Age Relaxation:
    • SC/ST: 5 years
    • OBC: 3 years
    • Ex-Servicemen: 3 years after deduction of military service rendered from actual age
    • Departmental Candidates (Central Govt. Employees): Up to 40 years (UR), 43 years (OBC), 45 years (SC/ST)
    • Candidates domiciled in J&K and Ladakh during 01-01-1980 to 31-12-1989 (UR): 5 years; (OBC): 8 years; (SC/ST): 10 years
    • Children and dependents of victims killed in 1984 riots (UR): 5 years; (OBC): 8 years; (SC/ST): 10 years
  • Age relaxation is NOT admissible to sons, daughters and dependents of ex-servicemen.
  • Date of Birth (D.O.B.) and name as recorded in Matriculation certificate will be accepted for determining the age and name. No subsequent request for change in date of birth and name will be considered.

Application Fee

  • UR, EWS and OBC Candidates: ₹100/- (Rupees One Hundred only) – Non-Refundable
  • Exempted (Fee = Nil): SC, ST, Ex-Servicemen and Female candidates are exempted from payment of examination fees.
  • Payment Mode: Net Banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI
  • No amount will be refunded in any case. Candidates are advised to avoid making multiple payments against a single registration as payments are non-refundable.

Important Dates

Event Date
Advertisement Published in Employment News 21–27 March 2026
Last Date for Receipt of Online Applications 30 days from date of publication of advertisement in Employment News
Crucial Date for Age & Eligibility Determination Closing date for receipt of applications (30 days from publication)

Selection Process

  • Stage I – Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Standard Test (PST):
    • PET – Male: 1.6 Km run in 6 Minutes 30 Seconds; Female: 800 Meter race in 4 minutes.
    • Ex-Servicemen are exempted from PET but must appear in PST, Written Examination, Skill Test (as applicable), Documentation and Medical Examination.
    • PST – Candidates must meet prescribed height and chest standards (post-wise and category-wise as per notification).
    • General (UR/EWS/OBC): Male – 165 cm height, 75–80 cm chest; Female – 155 cm height (Head Constable Steward). Different standards apply for SC/ST, NE States, J&K and other specified categories.
  • Stage II – Written Examination (Computer Based Test – CBT):
    • Paper-I Common Entrance Test: 3 hours duration, 150 marks, 150 multiple-choice objective type questions.
    • Part-I (50 Marks): General Knowledge, Mathematics, Reasoning, General English/General Hindi.
    • Part-II (100 Marks): Technical Subject.
    • Minimum qualifying marks: 50% for Gen/EWS/OBC/Ex-SM; 45% for SC/ST.
    • Final Merit will be prepared on the basis of marks obtained by candidates in the written examination.
  • Stage III – Skill Test (Only for Head Constable Electrician):
    • Skill test will be conducted for up to 20 times the vacancies (category-wise) for the post of Head Constable (Electrician).
    • Skill test carries 50 marks; minimum qualifying marks for all categories including SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen: 60%.
    • Skill test is qualifying in nature. Marks of skill test will not be included in the final merit list.
  • Stage IV – Document Verification (DV) and Detailed Medical Examination (DME) / Review Medical Examination (RME):
    • Candidates shortlisted up to 3 times the vacancies (category-wise) will be called for Documentation and DME/RME.
    • All original documents will be verified by detailed BOOs of SSB.
    • Candidates declared unfit in DME may prefer an appeal for Review Medical Examination (RME). The decision of RME Board will be final and no 2nd appeal will be entertained.
  • Final Selection: Final selection list will be prepared in order of merit, category-wise, after completion of Review Medical Examination. Where equal marks are obtained: candidate with more marks in Part-II (Technical Subject) will rank higher; if still equal, senior in age will rank higher; if date of birth is also same, priority based on alphabetical order of names in English.

General Information / Instructions

  • Posts are temporary but likely to continue. Selected candidates are liable to serve anywhere in India or outside the Territory of India and will be governed by SSB Act & Rules and other Rules amended from time to time.
  • A candidate can apply for only one post from amongst the posts mentioned in the notification.
  • Applications received through any mode except Online Mode shall not be accepted and rejected straightway.
  • SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates who fulfill eligibility criteria of unreserved category may apply against the unreserved category for posts where no vacancies are reserved for SC/ST/OBC/EWS.
  • OBC Non-Creamy Layer status should have been obtained within three years of the closing date of receipt of applications.
  • EWS certificate should be valid for 2026-27 as per Annexure-III. Family gross annual income must be below ₹8,00,000/-.
  • Biometrics of both thumbs (Left & Right) of candidates will be taken for verification in all stages after attendance.
  • Persons employed in Government/Semi-Government/Public Sector Undertakings should apply through proper channel and submit NOC from their employer at the time of documentation.
  • Name, Father's Name & Date of Birth should be mentioned exactly as recorded in the Matriculation certificate.
  • Candidates should be prepared for a stay of at least one week at the recruitment venue at their own arrangement. No TA/DA will be admissible.
  • Unemployed SC/ST candidates appearing in the written test will be reimbursed fare as admissible under relevant Rules subject to production of Rail/Bus tickets, original caste certificate and Non-employment certificate issued by MP or MLA or any Gazetted Officer.
  • Persons with bigamy/marriage to a person with a living spouse are disqualified. Conviction by court of law or dismissal from Government service also leads to disqualification.
  • Calculator, Digital Diary, Cellular Phone, pager, Whiteners, blade etc. are prohibited in the recruitment venue.
  • The DG, SSB has full right to make changes/cancel/postpone the recruitment without assigning any reason.
  • There will be no provision of reserve merit list/panel. If vacancies remain unfilled after declaration of final result, the same will be carried forward for the next recruitment.
  • Any amendment in the schedule/condition/process of recruitment will be available on SSB official website https://recruitment.ssb.gov.in/ & https://ssb.gov.in/ only.

How to Apply

  • Candidates are advised to go through the instructions and detailed advertisement available on SSB official website https://recruitment.ssb.gov.in/ & https://ssb.gov.in/ carefully before filling up the application form.
  • Applications must be submitted through Online Mode Only. Applications received through any other mode shall not be accepted.
  • Fill all fields in the application form including: Post applied, Candidate's Name, Father's/Husband's Name, Mother's Name, Date of Birth (in DD/MM/YYYY format as per matriculation certificate), Gender, Nationality, Religion, Marital Status, Category, Education Qualification, Postal/Permanent Address, Aadhaar Number (or valid Photo Identity Card number), Identification Marks, Mobile Number, E-mail ID, Criminal Cases Declaration.
  • Upload passport size coloured photograph in JPG format (less than 12 KB and greater than 4 KB, resolution 100 pixel width × 120 pixel height, white background with 75% face visible).
  • Upload signature in JPG format (less than 12 KB and greater than 4 KB, resolution 140 pixel width × 60 pixel height).
  • Pay the requisite fee of ₹100/- (for UR/EWS/OBC) through Net Banking / Credit Card / Debit Card / UPI. SC/ST/Ex-Servicemen/Women candidates are exempted from fee payment.
  • After successful submission, note down the Registration Number generated and take a printout of the application form/acknowledgement for future reference.
  • Application form can be edited by clicking "Edit Details" button till the closing/last date of receipt of online application form. After submission, no request for change/correction will be entertained.
  • Reporting to Recruitment Venue: Admit Cards can be downloaded from the SSB Recruitment website. Candidates must report to the venue on the date mentioned in the Admit Card with all original documents including Identity certificate, educational qualification certificates, Caste Certificates, Aadhaar Card etc.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Looking for SSF Constable (Barber) and Constable (Washerman) recruitment 2026? The Secretariat Security Force (SSF), Ministry of Home Affairs, invites online applications from eligible male candidates for 3 Constable (Barber) and 7 Constable (Washerman) posts. These entry-level roles in the Secretariat Security Force provide secure government jobs with pay scale Rs. 19,900-63,200 (Level-2, 7th CPC), trade-specific duties, and growth opportunities. Applications open from March 30, 2026, onwards via the official portal-check eligibility and apply early to secure your spot in this prestigious central force

SSF Constable Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF

Candidates who are interested in applying for the SSF Recruitment 2026 should first download and carefully read the official notification PDF. The notification contains complete details about vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, stipend/salary, selection process, important dates, and application instructions. Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form.

Download SSF Constable Job 2026 Notification PDF

SSF Constable Barber Washerman Recruitment 2026 Overview

Company Name Secretariat Security Force (SSF), Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India
Post Name Constable (Barber), Constable (Washerman)
No of Posts 10
Advt No CBC 19143/11/0016/2526
Salary Pay Scale Level-2 in Pay Matrix: ₹19,900 – ₹63,200/- Per Month (as per 7th CPC)
Qualification Matriculation pass or equivalent from a recognized School or Institution/Board + Must pass Trade Test in the profession
Age Limit 18 to 23 Years (as on closing date for receipt of online applications)
Start Date for Apply 30-03-2026 (00:01 AM)
Last Date for Apply 28-04-2026 (11:59 PM)
Official Website recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in

SSF Constable Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date / Period
Online Application Start Date March 30, 2026 (from 00:01 AM)
Online Application Closing Date April 28, 2026 (up to 11:59 PM)
Crucial Date for Eligibility (Age & Education) As on closing date of applications
Admit Card Download To be notified on recruitment portal
Selection Process Stages PET/PST, Written Exam, Trade Test, Document Verification, Medical Exam

SSF Constable Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details 

Post Name Total Vacancies Gender
Constable (Barber) 03 Male only
Constable (Washerman) 07 Male only
  • The vacancies are tentative and may increase or decrease without any notice. Any change in the number of vacancies will be intimated through ITBPF Recruitment website i.e. https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in.
  • 10% of the vacancies are reserved for Ex-Servicemen in the respective Post/Category.

SSF Constable Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

  • Qualifications – Constable (Barber):
    • Matriculation pass or equivalent from a recognized School or Institution/Board.
    • Candidates will have to pass a Trade Test in the profession (Barber).
  • Qualifications – Constable (Washerman):
    • Matriculation pass or equivalent from a recognized School or Institution/Board.
    • Candidates will have to pass a Trade Test in the profession (Washerman).
  • Nationality: Only eligible Male Indian citizens are invited to apply.
  • Crucial Date: All eligibility conditions (age, qualification etc.) will be reckoned as on the closing date for receipt of online applications i.e. 28-04-2026.
  • Candidates are advised to check their eligibility carefully before applying, so as to avoid disappointment at a later stage.
  • For detailed information about age relaxation, eligibility conditions, procedure of filling online application form, recruitment procedure, tests and pay & allowances, candidates are advised to go through the detailed advertisement on the ITBPF recruitment website https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in.

Salary / Stipend

  • Post: Constable (Barber) and Constable (Washerman)
  • Group: General Central Service, Group 'C' Non-Gazetted (Non-Ministerial)
  • Pay Scale (7th CPC): Level-2 in the Pay Matrix – ₹19,900 to ₹63,200/- Per Month
  • Posts are on temporary basis likely to be permanent in Secretariat Security Force (SSF).

Age Limit (as on Closing Date – 28-04-2026)

  • Constable (Barber): Between 18 to 23 Years
  • Constable (Washerman): Between 18 to 23 Years
  • Age relaxation details are available on the ITBPF recruitment website https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in.

Application Fee

  • General / OBC / EWS / Other Candidates: ₹100/- (Rupees One Hundred only)
  • Exempted (Fee = Nil): Ex-Servicemen and candidates belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) category are exempted from payment of application fee.
  • Payment Mode: Online through the ITBPF recruitment website https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in.

SSF Constable Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

  • Stage 1 – Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Candidates will be required to qualify the Physical Efficiency Test as prescribed for SSF recruitment.
  • Stage 2 – Physical Standard Test (PST): Physical Standard Test will be conducted to verify the physical measurements of candidates.
  • Stage 3 – Written Examination: Candidates who qualify PET and PST will appear in the Written Examination.
  • Stage 4 – Trade Test: Candidates must pass the Trade Test in their respective profession (Barber/Washerman).
  • Stage 5 – Verification of Original Documents: Original documents of shortlisted candidates will be verified.
  • Stage 6 – Detailed Medical Examination (DME): Shortlisted candidates will undergo Detailed Medical Examination.
  • Stage 7 – Review Medical Examination (RME): Candidates declared unfit in DME may apply for Review Medical Examination.
  • Medical Standards: Medical Examination to assess fitness will be conducted in terms of Uniform Guidelines for Recruitment Medical Examination for GOs and NGOs in CAPFs and ARs issued vide MHA U.O. No. A.VI-1/2014-Rectt (SSB) dated 20.05.2015 and A.VI.1/14-Rectt(SSB) dated 24.08.2015 and as amended from time to time by the Government.

SSF Constable Recruitment 2026 How to Apply

  • Visit the official recruitment website: https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in.
  • Online Application Mode will be opened w.e.f. 30th March 2026 at 00:01 AM and will be closed on 28th April 2026 at 11:59 PM.
  • Fill in all required details in the online application form carefully including personal details, educational qualification, category and other required information.
  • Upload the required documents and photograph/signature as per the specifications mentioned in the detailed advertisement on the ITBPF recruitment website.
  • Pay the application fee of ₹100/- online (exempted for Ex-Servicemen and SC category candidates).
  • Submit the online application form before the closing date and time. Take a printout of the submitted application form for future reference.
  • Candidates are advised to go through the detailed advertisement available on the ITBPF recruitment website https://recruitment.itbpolice.nic.in for complete instructions on eligibility conditions, procedure of filling online application form, recruitment procedure, tests, pay & allowances etc.

Class  Eligibility 
Primary Level Teachers (Class 1 to 5)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates are also eligible. In general, a minimum of 50% marks in graduation or the relevant training program is required.
Upper Primary Teacher (Class 6 to 8)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (45% in some cases as per rules) from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as B.Ed, BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates of these courses are also eligible. Additionally, candidates with specialized education qualifications (like Special Education) from RCI-recognized institutes are eligible. In some cases, candidates with 50% marks in intermediate and a 4-year integrated teaching program can also apply.

Short Details :Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission (UPESSC) has released the notification for the Uttar Pradesh Teacher Eligibility Test (UPTET) 2026. The UPTET application form has started from 27 March 2026, and the last date to apply for the UPTET Online Form 2026 is 26 April 2026. Meanwhile, UPESSC has also announced the exam dates. The UPTET examination will be conducted on 02, 03, and 04 July 2026. Candidates who are preparing for the UPTET 2026 examination can check all the important information related to the UPTET Online Form 2026 given below.

OnlyJobs Home page

Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission (UPESSC)

UPTET Online Form 2026

Important Dates

  • Application Start Date : 27 March 2026
  • Last Date : 26 April 2026
  • Fee Payment Last Date : 26 April 2026
  • Correction Last Date : 01 May 2026
  • Complete Form/ Submit Date : Available Now
  • Exam Date : 2, 3, & 4 July 2026
  • Admit Card : Before Exam

Application Fee

  • Fees For Single Paper
  • General, EWS, OBC : Rs. 1000/-
  • SC, ST : Rs. 500/-
  • PH Candidates : Rs. 300/-
  • Candidates have to pay their Examination Fee Through Debit Card / Credit Card / Net Banking / UPI and Other Fee Payment Mode Only.

UPTET Notification 2026 : Age Limit

  • As per rules UPTET
  • Use Tool –

UPTET Online Form 2026 : Educational Qualification 

Class  Eligibility 
Primary Level Teachers (Class 1 to 5)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates are also eligible. In general, a minimum of 50% marks in graduation or the relevant training program is required.
Upper Primary Teacher (Class 6 to 8)
  • Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (45% in some cases as per rules) from an NCTE-recognized institution along with a valid teacher training qualification such as B.Ed, BTC, B.El.Ed, or equivalent. Final-year candidates of these courses are also eligible. Additionally, candidates with specialized education qualifications (like Special Education) from RCI-recognized institutes are eligible. In some cases, candidates with 50% marks in intermediate and a 4-year integrated teaching program can also apply.

How To Apply For UPTET Online Form 2026

  • Candidates can apply through link https://upessc.up.gov.in/ or provided below in link section or they can also apply through official site of the UPTET Online Form before Last Date.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 has been released by the Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) for CEN 08/2024 on the official portal rrb.digialm.com and regional RRB websites, along with candidates’ response sheets and the online objection facility.

RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 – Overview

The RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 is the official set of correct answers for the Group D Computer-Based Test (CBT), published online by Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) to help candidates check their responses and estimate scores before the result. It is especially important for candidates who appeared in the CBT from 27 November 2025 to 10 February 2026, as they can verify question-wise accuracy, understand likely cut off scenarios, and decide whether to raise objections within the given window.

Quick Overview Table

  • Organization Name: Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs).
  • Exam Name: RRB Group D CBT 2026 (CEN 08/2024 Level 1 posts).
  • Post Name: Group D / Level 1 posts in Indian Railways.
  • Answer Key Status: Provisional answer key and response sheet released; objection window open.
  • Answer Key Release Date: 17 February 2026
  • Objection Window: Open until 23 February 2026 (11:59 p.m.) with online fee payment per question.
  • Official Website: rrb.digialm.com and regional RRB portals such as rrbcdg.gov.in.

Important Official Links

  • RRB Group D Answer Key Notice and Response Sheet Login (CEN 08/2024)
  • Main Answer Key / Response Sheet Login Page

RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 Release Date

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) uploaded the official notice for RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 on 17 February 2026 and activated the response sheet and answer key download link that day at 5:00 p.m. on rrb.digialm.com and regional sites. The provisional answer key has been released shortly after completion of all CBT shifts, with more than a million candidates now able to log in, check their question paper, and see the official responses. Candidates must use this window to check every question carefully because the same answer key, after resolving objections, will form the basis of the final result.

Direct Links

  • RRB Answer Key Official Notice and DigiALM Login for CEN 08/2024

How to Download RRB Group D Answer Key 2026

Use this step-by-step process to download the answer key, question paper, and response sheet:

  1. Go to the official RRB CBT portal at rrb.digialm.com or open your regional RRB website (such as rrbcdg.gov.in) and click on the CEN 08/2024 Group D answer key link.
  2. On the answer key page, select the link titled “RRB Group D Answer Key / Question Paper / Response Sheet 2026” for CEN 08/2024.
  3. Enter your User ID/Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth exactly as used during registration or admit card download.
  4. After login, your response sheet will open, showing each question, your marked option, and the official correct option from the provisional answer key.
  5. Use the download/print button to save your answer key, response sheet and question paper as a PDF file for score calculation and future reference.
  6. If you intend to challenge any answer, note the Question ID and option details from this screen before visiting the objection section.

Direct Login Link: RRB Group D Answer Key Login at rrb.digialm.com (CEN 08/2024)

Details Mentioned on RRB Group D Response Sheet

The RRB Group D response sheet and answer key display the following key details that you should verify:

  • Candidate information (name or masked ID) and exam details such as date, shift and exam city/centre.
  • Question ID and question number for every item in the paper.
  • Status of each question (answered, not answered, marked for review).
  • Option chosen by the candidate and whether it is correct or incorrect as per the provisional key.
  • Official correct answer marked separately for each question.

These details help ensure that you are viewing the correct paper and can accurately calculate your approximate marks.

How to Raise Objection Against RRB Group D Answer Key

If you believe any answer in the provisional RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 is incorrect, you can raise an objection during the active window:

  1. Log in again at rrb.digialm.com or the objection link provided on your regional RRB website using your registration credentials.
  2. Click on the “Objection Tracker / Challenge Answer Key” option for CEN 08/2024.
  3. Select the Question ID you want to challenge and specify the correct option you believe should replace the provisional answer.
  4. Upload supporting evidence (e.g., standard textbooks, official documents or other authoritative references) as per the prescribed format.
  5. Pay the objection fee of ₹50 per question (plus bank charges), which is refundable if the objection is accepted by RRB.
  6. Submit your objections before 23 February 2026, after which the objection window will close and late submissions will not be considered.

RRBs will examine all valid objections, update the key where required, and publish a final answer key that will be used to prepare the Group D result.

Objection Fee and Answer Key Timeline

Activity Date/Details
Provisional answer key & response sheet release 17 February 2026
Objection window start 17 February 2026 (with answer key)
Objection window closing 23 February 2026 (11:59 p.m.)
Objection fee ₹50 per question, refundable if valid

  • Download Answer Key: Click Here
  • Raise Objection: Objection Tracker via the login portal above (after accessing response sheet).
  • Check Result: RRB Group D Result 2026 (to be updated after declaration) on regional RRB websites.
  • Download Admit Card: Archived RRB Group D CBT admit card login page (via regional sites).
  • Official Website: Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) – rrb.digialm.com and regional sites like rrbcdg.gov.in.

UP Police Sub Inspector Recruitment 2025 : Educational Qualification

  • Candidates must hold a Graduation (Bachelor’s) degree from a recognized university or institution in India or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government.
  • Important points : 
  • It is mandatory to upload the graduation mark sheet.
  • If the graduation degree (certificate) is not available, then the provisional graduation degree must be uploaded.
  • If the provisional graduation degree is also not available, the candidate should upload their mark sheets in its place.
  • During document verification and physical standard test, it will be mandatory for the candidates to present the original graduation degree (certificate) along with the graduation mark sheets.
  • Preference Qualification (Not Mandatory) :
  • Passing the “O” Level Computer Examination conducted by NIELIT (formerly DOEACC) or an equivalent, recognized certification
  • A minimum of two years of service in the Territorial Army.
  • A “B” certificate from the National Cadet Corps (NCC).
  • All candidates must read the official information completely before applying.

UP Police Sub Inspector Recruitment 2025 : Educational Qualification

  • Candidates must hold a Graduation (Bachelor’s) degree from a recognized university or institution in India or an equivalent qualification recognized by the Government.
  • Important points : 
  • It is mandatory to upload the graduation mark sheet.
  • If the graduation degree (certificate) is not available, then the provisional graduation degree must be uploaded.
  • If the provisional graduation degree is also not available, the candidate should upload their mark sheets in its place.
  • During document verification and physical standard test, it will be mandatory for the candidates to present the original graduation degree (certificate) along with the graduation mark sheets.
  • Preference Qualification (Not Mandatory) :
  • Passing the “O” Level Computer Examination conducted by NIELIT (formerly DOEACC) or an equivalent, recognized certification
  • A minimum of two years of service in the Territorial Army.
  • A “B” certificate from the National Cadet Corps (NCC).
  • All candidates must read the official information completely before applying.
